=============================================================================\n17\t// FIELD REFERENCE FORMATTING\n18\t// =============================================================================\n19\t\n20\t// FormatFieldRef creates a standardized field reference string with optional parent.\n21\t// This ensures field names are consistently formatted across error messages.\n22\t// Supports nested field paths and array index formatting.\n23\t//\n24\t// Parameters:\n25\t//   - fieldName: The name of the field (e.g., \"email\", \"user.address\", \"users.0.email\")\n26\t//   - parent: Optional parent path (e.g., \"request\", \"response.body\")\n27\t//\n28\t// Returns a formatted field reference string with array indices in bracket notation.\n29\t// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" for consistent error display.\n30\t//\n31\t// Example usage:\n32\t//\n33\t//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"email\", \"request\")\n34\t//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\n35\t//\n36\t//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"email\", \"\")\n37\t//\t// Returns: \"email\"\n38\t//\n39\t//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"users.0.email\", \"response\")\n40\t//\t// Returns: \"response.users[0].email\"\n41\t//\n42\t//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"\", \"\")\n43\t//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\n44\tfunc FormatFieldRef(fieldName string, parent string) string {\n45\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n46\t\tparent = strings.TrimSpace(parent)\n47\t\n48\t\tif fieldName == \"\" {\n49\t\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n50\t\t\t\t// Normalize parent path even when field is empty\n51\t\t\t\tnormalizedParent := FormatFieldPath(parent)\n52\t\t\t\tif normalizedParent != \"(unknown field)\" {\n53\t\t\t\t\treturn normalizedParent\n54\t\t\t\t}\n55\t\t\t\treturn parent\n56\t\t\t}\n57\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n58\t\t}\n59\t\n60\t\t// Normalize field name to handle array indices (e.g., \"users.0.email\" → \"users[0].email\")\n61\t\tnormalizedFieldName := FormatFieldPath(fieldName)\n62\t\n63\t\t// Check if normalization resulted in unknown field indicator\n64\t\tif normalizedFieldName == \"(unknown field)\" {\n65\t\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n66\t\t\t\treturn parent\n67\t\t\t}\n68\t\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n69\t\t}\n70\t\n71\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n72\t\t\t// Normalize parent as well in case it contains array indices\n73\t\t\tnormalizedParent := FormatFieldPath(parent)\n74\t\n75\t\t\t// If parent normalizes to unknown field, just return the field name\n76\t\t\tif normalizedParent == \"(unknown field)\" {\n77\t\t\t\treturn normalizedFieldName\n78\t\t\t}\n79\t\n80\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s.%s\", normalizedParent, normalizedFieldName)\n81\t\t}\n82\t\n83\t\treturn normalizedFieldName\n84\t}\n85\t\n86\t// FormatFieldLocationWith creates a detailed field location string with context.\n87\t// This is useful for error messages that need to show exactly where an error occurred.\n88\t// Unlike FormatFieldLocation in format_helpers.go, this supports parent paths.\n89\t//\n90\t// Parameters:\n91\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n92\t//   - location: Additional location context (e.g., \"line 5\", \"position 123\")\n93\t//   - parent: Optional parent path\n94\t//\n95\t// Returns a formatted location string.\n96\t//\n97\t// Example usage:\n98\t//\n99\t//\tloc := FormatFieldLocationWith(\"email\", \"line 5\", \"request\")\n100\t//\t// Returns: \"request.email at line 5\"\n101\t//\n102\t//\tloc := FormatFieldLocationWith(\"email\", \"\", \"request\")\n103\t//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\n104\tfunc FormatFieldLocationWith(fieldName string, location string, parent string) string {\n105\t\tref := FormatFieldRef(fieldName, parent)\n106\t\tlocation = strings.TrimSpace(location)\n107\t\n108\t\tif location != \"\" {\n109\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s at %s\", ref, location)\n110\t\t}\n111\t\n112\t\treturn ref\n113\t}\n114\t\n115\t// FormatFieldListWith formats a list of field names into a readable string with conjunction.\n116\t// Unlike FormatFieldList in format_helpers.go, this supports custom conjunctions.\n117\t//\n118\t// Parameters:\n119\t//   - fields: Slice of field names\n120\t//   - conjunction: The conjunction to use (e.g., \"and\", \"or\")\n121\t//\n122\t// Returns a formatted list string.\n123\t//\n124\t// Example usage:\n125\t//\n126\t//\tlist := FormatFieldListWith([]string{\"email\", \"password\"}, \"and\")\n127\t//\t// Returns: \"email and password\"\n128\t//\n129\t//\tlist := FormatFieldListWith([]string{\"email\", \"password\", \"name\"}, \"and\")\n130\t//\t// Returns: \"email, password, and name\"\n131\tfunc FormatFieldListWith(fields []string, conjunction string) string {\n132\t\tif len(fields) == 0 {\n133\t\t\treturn \"\"\n134\t\t}\n135\t\n136\t\tif len(fields) == 1 {\n137\t\t\treturn fields[0]\n138\t\t}\n139\t\n140\t\tif len(fields) == 2 {\n141\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s %s %s\", fields[0], conjunction, fields[1])\n142\t\t}\n143\t\n144\t\t// Oxford comma style\n145\t\tallButLast := fields[:len(fields)-1]\n146\t\tlast := fields[len(fields)-1]\n147\t\n148\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s, %s %s\", strings.Join(allButLast, \", \"), conjunction, last)\n149\t}\n150\t\n151\t// FormatFieldReferenceWithType creates a field reference with ErrorType context.\n152\t// This provides type-specific field reference formatting that includes\n153\t// contextual information based on the error type.\n154\t//\n155\t// Parameters:\n156\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n157\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n158\t//   - parent: Optional parent path\n159\t//\n160\t// Returns a formatted field reference string with error type context.\n161\t//\n162\t// Example usage:\n163\t//\n164\t//\tref := FormatFieldReferenceWithType(ErrTypeRequired, \"email\", \"request\")\n165\t//\t// Returns: \"[Required Field] request.email\"\n166\t//\n167\t//\tref := FormatFieldReferenceWithType(ErrTypeFormat, \"email\", \"\")\n168\t//\t// Returns: \"[Format Error] email\"\n169\tfunc FormatFieldReferenceWithType(errorType ErrorType, fieldName string, parent string) string {\n170\t\tfieldRef := FormatFieldRef(fieldName, parent)\n171\t\terrorTypeLabel := FormatErrorType(errorType)\n172\t\n173\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] %s\", errorTypeLabel, fieldRef)\n174\t}\n175\t\n176\t// FormatFieldReferenceWithCategory creates a field reference with category context.\n177\t// This formats field references with their error category for better context.\n178\t//\n179\t// Parameters:\n180\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n181\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n182\t//   - parent: Optional parent path\n183\t//\n184\t// Returns a formatted field reference string with category context.\n185\t//\n186\t// Example usage:\n187\t//\n188\t//\tref := FormatFieldReferenceWithCategory(ErrTypeRequired, \"email\", \"request\")\n189\t//\t// Returns: \"[Data Validation] request.email\"\n190\t//\n191\t//\tref := FormatFieldReferenceWithCategory(ErrTypeFormat, \"email\", \"\")\n192\t//\t// Returns: \"[Data Validation] email\"\n193\tfunc FormatFieldReferenceWithCategory(errorType ErrorType, fieldName string, parent string) string {\n194\t\tfieldRef := FormatFieldRef(fieldName, parent)\n195\t\tcategory := GetCategoryForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n196\t\tcategoryLabel := FormatCategory(category)\n197\t\n198\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] %s\", categoryLabel, fieldRef)\n199\t}\n200\t\n201\t// =============================================================================\n202\t// ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\n203\t// =============================================================================\n204\t\n205\t// FormatErrorMessage creates a standardized error message from components.\n206\t// This ensures error messages are consistently formatted.\n207\t//\n208\t// Parameters:\n209\t//   - errorType: The type of error (use ErrorType enum or string)\n210\t//   - message: The core error message\n211\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n212\t//\n213\t// Returns a formatted error message string.\n214\t//\n215\t// Example usage:\n216\t//\n217\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessage(\"required\", \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n218\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: Field is required\"\n219\t//\n220\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessage(\"format\", \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n221\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n222\tfunc FormatErrorMessage(errorType string, message string, fieldName string) string {\n223\t\terrorType = strings.TrimSpace(errorType)\n224\t\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n225\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n226\t\n227\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n228\t\n229\t\t// Add error type in brackets\n230\t\tif errorType != \"\" {\n231\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", errorType))\n232\t\t}\n233\t\n234\t\t// Add field name if present\n235\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n236\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n237\t\t}\n238\t\n239\t\t// Add message\n240\t\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n241\t\n242\t\treturn builder.String()\n243\t}\n244\t\n245\t// FormatErrorMessageWithType creates a standardized error message using ErrorType enum.\n246\t// This provides type-safe error type specification and consistent formatting.\n247\t//\n248\t// Parameters:\n249\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n250\t//   - message: The core error message\n251\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n252\t//\n253\t// Returns a formatted error message string.\n254\t//\n255\t// Example usage:\n256\t//\n257\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessageWithType(ErrTypeRequired, \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n258\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: Field is required\"\n259\t//\n260\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessageWithType(ErrTypeFormat, \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n261\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n262\tfunc FormatErrorMessageWithType(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n263\t\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorType.String(), message, fieldName)\n264\t}\n265\t\n266\t// FormatErrorWithSeverity creates an error message with severity information.\n267\t// This integrates ErrorType enum with automatic severity lookup.\n268\t//\n269\t// Parameters:\n270\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n271\t//   - message: The core error message\n272\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n273\t//\n274\t// Returns a formatted error message string with severity prefix.\n275\t//\n276\t// Example usage:\n277\t//\n278\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithSeverity(ErrTypeRequired, \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n279\t//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] High [required] email: Field is required\"\n280\t//\n281\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithSeverity(ErrTypeFormat, \"Invalid format\", \"email\")\n282\t//\t// Returns: \"[■] Medium [format] email: Invalid format\"\n283\tfunc FormatErrorWithSeverity(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n284\t\tseverity := GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n285\t\tseverityLabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)\n286\t\terrorTypeLabel := errorType.String()\n287\t\n288\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n289\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s [%s] \", severityLabel, errorTypeLabel))\n290\t\n291\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n292\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n293\t\t}\n294\t\n295\t\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n296\t\n297\t\treturn builder.String()\n298\t}\n299\t\n300\t// FormatErrorWithValues creates an error message showing expected vs actual values.\n301\t// This is useful for validation errors where values don't match expectations.\n302\t//\n303\t// Parameters:\n304\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n305\t//   - expected: The expected value\n306\t//   - actual: The actual value\n307\t//   - customMessage: Optional custom message prefix\n308\t//\n309\t// Returns a formatted error message with value comparison.\n310\t//\n311\t// Example usage:\n312\t//\n313\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithValues(\"status_code\", 200, 404, \"\")\n314\t//\t// Returns: \"status_code: expected 200, got 404\"\n315\t//\n316\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithValues(\"count\", 5, 3, \"Not enough items\")\n317\t//\t// Returns: \"count: Not enough items (expected 5, got 3)\"\n318\tfunc FormatErrorWithValues(fieldName string, expected interface{}, actual interface{}, customMessage string) string {\n319\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n320\t\tcustomMessage = strings.TrimSpace(customMessage)\n321\t\n322\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n323\t\n324\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n325\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n326\t\t}\n327\t\n328\t\tif customMessage != \"\" {\n329\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", customMessage))\n330\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"(expected %v, got %v)\", expected, actual))\n331\t\t} else {\n332\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected %v, got %v\", expected, actual))\n333\t\t}\n334\t\n335\t\treturn builder.String()\n336\t}\n337\t\n338\t// FormatErrorWithRange creates an error message for range validation failures.\n339\t// This is useful for numeric range validations or length validations.\n340\t//\n341\t// Parameters:\n342\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n343\t//   - minValue: The minimum allowed value\n344\t//   - maxValue: The maximum allowed value\n345\t//   - actualValue: The actual value that failed validation\n346\t//\n347\t// Returns a formatted range error message.\n348\t//\n349\t// Example usage:\n350\t//\n351\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithRange(\"age\", 0, 120, 150)\n352\t//\t// Returns: \"age: value 150 is outside valid range [0, 120]\"\n353\tfunc FormatErrorWithRange(fieldName string, minValue interface{}, maxValue interface{}, actualValue interface{}) string {\n354\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n355\t\n356\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n357\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: value %v is outside valid range [%v, %v]\",\n358\t\t\t\tfieldName, actualValue, minValue, maxValue)\n359\t\t}\n360\t\n361\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"value %v is outside valid range [%v, %v]\",\n362\t\t\tactualValue, minValue, maxValue)\n363\t}\n364\t\n365\t// FormatErrorWithPattern creates an error message for pattern validation failures.\n366\t// This is useful for format validations like email, URL, regex patterns.\n367\t//\n368\t// Parameters:\n369\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n370\t//   - pattern: The pattern that failed (can be a description)\n371\t//   - actualValue: The actual value that failed validation\n372\t//\n373\t// Returns a formatted pattern error message.\n374\t//\n375\t// Example usage:\n376\t//\n377\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithPattern(\"email\", \"must contain @\", \"invalidemail\")\n378\t//\t// Returns: \"email: value 'invalidemail' does not match pattern (must contain @)\"\n379\tfunc FormatErrorWithPattern(fieldName string, pattern string, actualValue interface{}) string {\n380\t\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n381\t\tpattern = strings.TrimSpace(pattern)\n382\t\n383\t\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n384\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: value '%v' does not match pattern (%s)\",\n385\t\t\t\tfieldName, actualValue, pattern)\n386\t\t}\n387\t\n388\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"value '%v' does not match pattern (%s)\",\n389\t\t\tactualValue, pattern)\n390\t}\n391\t\n392\t// =============================================================================\n393\t// ERROR TYPE FORMATTING\n394\t// =============================================================================\n395\t\n396\t// FormatErrorType creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorType enum.\n397\t// This converts technical error type names to readable labels.\n398\t//\n399\t// Parameters:\n400\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n401\t//\n402\t// Returns a formatted error type label.\n403\t//\n404\t// Example usage:\n405\t//\n406\t//\tlabel := FormatErrorType(ErrTypeRequired)\n407\t//\t// Returns: \"Required Field\"\n408\t//\n409\t//\tlabel := FormatErrorType(ErrTypeFormat)\n410\t//\t// Returns: \"Format Error\"\n411\tfunc FormatErrorType(errorType ErrorType) string {\n412\t\tswitch errorType {\n413\t\tcase ErrTypeRequired:\n414\t\t\treturn \"Required Field\"\n415\t\tcase ErrTypeFormat:\n416\t\t\treturn \"Format Error\"\n417\t\tcase ErrTypeRange:\n418\t\t\treturn \"Range Error\"\n419\t\tcase ErrTypeLength:\n420\t\t\treturn \"Length Error\"\n421\t\tcase ErrTypeType:\n422\t\t\treturn \"Type Error\"\n423\t\tcase ErrTypeValue:\n424\t\t\treturn \"Invalid Value\"\n425\t\tcase ErrTypeDuplicate:\n426\t\t\treturn \"Duplicate Value\"\n427\t\tcase ErrTypeConflict:\n428\t\t\treturn \"Conflict\"\n429\t\tcase ErrTypeUnknown:\n430\t\t\treturn \"Unknown Error\"\n431\t\tdefault:\n432\t\t\treturn string(errorType)\n433\t\t}\n434\t}\n435\t\n436\t// FormatErrorTypeFrom creates a user-friendly label from a string error type.\n437\t// This converts technical error type strings to readable labels.\n438\t//\n439\t// Parameters:\n440\t//   - errorTypeStr: The error type string\n441\t//\n442\t// Returns a formatted error type label.\n443\t//\n444\t// Example usage:\n445\t//\n446\t//\tlabel := FormatErrorTypeFrom(\"required\")\n447\t//\t// Returns: \"Required Field\"\n448\t//\n449\t//\tlabel := FormatErrorTypeFrom(\"format\")\n450\t//\t// Returns: \"Format Error\"\n451\tfunc FormatErrorTypeFrom(errorTypeStr string) string {\n452\t\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(errorTypeStr)\n453\t\treturn FormatErrorType(et)\n454\t}\n455\t\n456\t// =============================================================================\n457\t// CATEGORY FORMATTING\n458\t// =============================================================================\n459\t\n460\t// FormatCategory creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorCategory.\n461\t// This converts technical category names to readable labels.\n462\t//\n463\t// Parameters:\n464\t//   - category: The ErrorCategory value\n465\t//\n466\t// Returns a formatted category label.\n467\t//\n468\t// Example usage:\n469\t//\n470\t//\tlabel := FormatCategory(CategoryHTTP)\n471\t//\t// Returns: \"HTTP Protocol\"\n472\t//\n473\t//\tlabel := FormatCategory(CategoryValidation)\n474\t//\t// Returns: \"Data Validation\"\n475\tfunc FormatCategory(category ErrorCategory) string {\n476\t\tswitch category {\n477\t\tcase CategoryHTTP:\n478\t\t\treturn \"HTTP Protocol\"\n479\t\tcase CategoryContent:\n480\t\t\treturn \"Content\"\n481\t\tcase CategoryValidation:\n482\t\t\treturn \"Data Validation\"\n483\t\tcase CategoryPerformance:\n484\t\t\treturn \"Performance\"\n485\t\tcase CategorySecurity:\n486\t\t\treturn \"Security\"\n487\t\tcase CategoryCustom:\n488\t\t\treturn \"Custom\"\n489\t\tdefault:\n490\t\t\treturn string(category)\n491\t\t}\n492\t}\n493\t\n494\t// FormatErrorByCategory creates a category-aware error message.\n495\t// This formats errors according to their category, including appropriate\n496\t// contextual information based on the error type's category.\n497\t//\n498\t// Parameters:\n499\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n500\t//   - message: The core error message\n501\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n502\t//\n503\t// Returns a formatted error message string with category context.\n504\t//\n505\t// Example usage:\n506\t//\n507\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorByCategory(ErrTypeRequired, \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n508\t//\t// Returns: \"[Data Validation] [required] email: Field is required\"\n509\t//\n510\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorByCategory(ErrTypeFormat, \"Invalid format\", \"email\")\n511\t//\t// Returns: \"[Data Validation] [format] email: Invalid format\"\n512\tfunc FormatErrorByCategory(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n513\t\tcategory := GetCategoryForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n514\t\tcategoryLabel := FormatCategory(category)\n515\t\n516\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n517\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", categoryLabel))\n518\t\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorMessageWithType(errorType, message, fieldName))\n519\t\n520\t\treturn builder.String()\n521\t}\n522\t\n523\t// FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity creates a comprehensive error message\n524\t// with both category and severity information.\n525\t//\n526\t// Parameters:\n527\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n528\t//   - message: The core error message\n529\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n530\t//\n531\t// Returns a formatted error message string with severity, category, and error type.\n532\t//\n533\t// Example usage:\n534\t//\n535\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(ErrTypeRequired, \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n536\t//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] High [Data Validation] [required] email: Field is required\"\n537\tfunc FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n538\t\tseverity := GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n539\t\tseverityLabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)\n540\t\tcategory := GetCategoryForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n541\t\tcategoryLabel := FormatCategory(category)\n542\t\n543\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n544\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s [%s] \", severityLabel, categoryLabel))\n545\t\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorMessageWithType(errorType, message, fieldName))\n546\t\n547\t\treturn builder.String()\n548\t}\n549\t\n550\t// =============================================================================\n551\t// SEVERITY FORMATTING\n552\t// =============================================================================\n553\t\n554\t// FormatSeverity creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorSeverity.\n555\t// This converts technical severity names to readable labels.\n556\t//\n557\t// Parameters:\n558\t//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity value\n559\t//\n560\t// Returns a formatted severity label.\n561\t//\n562\t// Example usage:\n563\t//\n564\t//\tlabel := FormatSeverity(SeverityCritical)\n565\t//\t// Returns: \"CRITICAL\"\n566\t//\n567\t//\tlabel := FormatSeverity(SeverityHigh)\n568\t//\t// Returns: \"High\"\n569\tfunc FormatSeverity(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n570\t\tswitch severity {\n571\t\tcase SeverityCritical:\n572\t\t\treturn \"CRITICAL\"\n573\t\tcase SeverityHigh:\n574\t\t\treturn \"High\"\n575\t\tcase SeverityMedium:\n576\t\t\treturn \"Medium\"\n577\t\tcase SeverityLow:\n578\t\t\treturn \"Low\"\n579\t\tcase SeverityInfo:\n580\t\t\treturn \"Info\"\n581\t\tdefault:\n582\t\t\treturn string(severity)\n583\t\t}\n584\t}\n585\t\n586\t// FormatSeverityWithIndicator adds visual indicators to severity labels.\n587\t// This is useful for console output or UI display.\n588\t//\n589\t// Parameters:\n590\t//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity value\n591\t//\n592\t// Returns a severity label with visual indicator.\n593\t//\n594\t// Example usage:\n595\t//\n596\t//\tlabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(SeverityCritical)\n597\t//\t// Returns: \"[!] CRITICAL\"\n598\t//\n599\t//\tlabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(SeverityHigh)\n600\t//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] High\"\n601\tfunc FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n602\t\tindicator := severityIndicator(severity)\n603\t\tlabel := FormatSeverity(severity)\n604\t\n605\t\tif indicator != \"\" {\n606\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] %s\", indicator, label)\n607\t\t}\n608\t\n609\t\treturn label\n610\t}\n611\t\n612\t// severityIndicator returns a visual indicator for a severity level.\n613\tfunc severityIndicator(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n614\t\tswitch severity {\n615\t\tcase SeverityCritical:\n616\t\t\treturn \"!\"\n617\t\tcase SeverityHigh:\n618\t\t\treturn \"⚠\"\n619\t\tcase SeverityMedium:\n620\t\t\treturn \"■\"\n621\t\tcase SeverityLow:\n622\t\t\treturn \"○\"\n623\t\tcase SeverityInfo:\n624\t\t\treturn \"i\"\n625\t\tdefault:\n626\t\t\treturn \"?\"\n627\t\t}\n628\t}\n629\t\n630\t// =============================================================================\n631\t// COMPREHENSIVE ERROR FORMATTING\n632\t// =============================================================================\n633\t\n634\t// FormatValidationErrorFull creates a comprehensive, formatted error message from a ValidationError.\n635\t// This combines all relevant error information into a single, readable message.\n636\t// Unlike FormatValidationError in format_helpers.go, this takes a ValidationError struct.\n637\t//\n638\t// Parameters:\n639\t//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n640\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n641\t//\n642\t// Returns a formatted error message string.\n643\t//\n644\t// Example usage:\n645\t//\n646\t//\terr := ValidationError{\n647\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n648\t//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n649\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n650\t//\t}\n651\t//\n652\t//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, true)\n653\t//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required\"\n654\tfunc FormatValidationErrorFull(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n655\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n656\t\n657\t\t// Add severity if requested\n658\t\tif includeSeverity {\n659\t\t\t// Try to get severity from ErrorType enum first\n660\t\t\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(err.ErrorType)\n661\t\t\tvar severity ErrorSeverity\n662\t\t\tif et.IsValid() && et != ErrTypeUnknown {\n663\t\t\t\tseverity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(et)\n664\t\t\t} else {\n665\t\t\t\t// Fall back to string-based error type lookup\n666\t\t\t\tseverity = GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(err.ErrorType)\n667\t\t\t}\n668\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)))\n669\t\t}\n670\t\n671\t\t// Add error type and field\n672\t\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorMessage(err.ErrorType, err.Message, err.FieldName))\n673\t\n674\t\t// Add expected/actual values if present\n675\t\tif err.Expected != nil || err.Actual != nil {\n676\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n677\t\t\tif err.Expected != nil {\n678\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %v\", err.Expected))\n679\t\t\t}\n680\t\t\tif err.Expected != nil && err.Actual != nil {\n681\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\", \")\n682\t\t\t}\n683\t\t\tif err.Actual != nil {\n684\t\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %v\", err.Actual))\n685\t\t\t}\n686\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n687\t\t}\n688\t\n689\t\t// Add location if present\n690\t\tif err.Location != \"\" {\n691\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" [%s]\", err.Location))\n692\t\t}\n693\t\n694\t\treturn builder.String()\n695\t}\n696\t\n697\t// FormatValidationErrorBrief creates a brief, one-line error message.\n698\t// This is useful for summaries and compact displays.\n699\t//\n700\t// Parameters:\n701\t//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n702\t//\n703\t// Returns a brief error message string.\n704\t//\n705\t// Example usage:\n706\t//\n707\t//\terr := ValidationError{\n708\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n709\t//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n710\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n711\t//\t}\n712\t//\n713\t//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorBrief(err)\n714\t//\t// Returns: \"email: required\"\n715\tfunc FormatValidationErrorBrief(err ValidationError) string {\n716\t\tif err.FieldName != \"\" {\n717\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %s\", err.FieldName, err.ErrorType)\n718\t\t}\n719\t\n720\t\treturn err.ErrorType\n721\t}\n722\t\n723\t// =============================================================================\n724\t// ERROR LIST FORMATTING\n725\t// =============================================================================\n726\t\n727\t// FormatErrorList formats multiple errors into a readable list.\n728\t// This is useful for displaying multiple validation failures.\n729\t//\n730\t// Parameters:\n731\t//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to format\n732\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n733\t//\n734\t// Returns a formatted error list string.\n735\t//\n736\t// Example usage:\n737\t//\n738\t//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n739\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n740\t//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n741\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n742\t//\t}, {\n743\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeFormat),\n744\t//\t    Message: \"Invalid format\",\n745\t//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n746\t//\t}}\n747\t//\n748\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorList(errors, true)\n749\t//\t// Returns:\n750\t//\t// 1. [High] [required] email: Field is required\n751\t//\t// 2. [High] [format] password: Invalid format\n752\tfunc FormatErrorList(errors []ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n753\t\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n754\t\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n755\t\t}\n756\t\n757\t\tvar lines []string\n758\t\tfor i, err := range errors {\n759\t\t\tprefix := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d. \", i+1)\n760\t\t\tformatted := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity)\n761\t\t\tlines = append(lines, prefix+formatted)\n762\t\t}\n763\t\n764\t\treturn strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n765\t}\n766\t\n767\t// FormatErrorListSummary creates a summary of multiple errors.\n768\t// This provides a high-level overview of validation failures.\n769\t//\n770\t// Parameters:\n771\t//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to summarize\n772\t//\n773\t// Returns a summary string with error counts by type.\n774\t//\n775\t// Example usage:\n776\t//\n777\t//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n778\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n779\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n780\t//\t}, {\n781\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n782\t//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n783\t//\t}}\n784\t//\n785\t//\tsummary := FormatErrorListSummary(errors)\n786\t//\t// Returns: \"2 errors: required (2)\"\n787\tfunc FormatErrorListSummary(errors []ValidationError) string {\n788\t\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n789\t\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n790\t\t}\n791\t\n792\t\t// Count errors by type\n793\t\tcounts := make(map[string]int)\n794\t\tfor _, err := range errors {\n795\t\t\tcounts[err.ErrorType]++\n796\t\t}\n797\t\n798\t\t// Build summary parts\n799\t\tvar parts []string\n800\t\tfor errorType, count := range counts {\n801\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%d)\", errorType, count))\n802\t\t}\n803\t\n804\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%d error(s): %s\", len(errors), strings.Join(parts, \", \"))\n805\t}\n806\t\n807\t// =============================================================================\n808\t// CONVENIENCE WRAPPER FUNCTIONS\n809\t// =============================================================================\n810\t\n811\t// FormatFieldLocation is a convenience wrapper for FormatFieldLocationWith\n812\t// without the parent parameter.\n813\t//\n814\t// Parameters:\n815\t//   - fieldName: The field name\n816\t//   - location: Additional location context\n817\t//\n818\t// Returns a formatted location string.\n819\t//\n820\t// Example usage:\n821\t//\n822\t//\tloc := FormatFieldLocation(\"email\", \"line 5\")\n823\t//\t// Returns: \"email at line 5\"\n824\tfunc FormatFieldLocation(fieldName string, location string) string {\n825\t\treturn FormatFieldLocationWith(fieldName, location, \"\")\n826\t}\n827\t\n828\t// FormatFieldList is a convenience wrapper for FormatFieldListWith\n829\t// with \"and\" as the default conjunction.\n830\t//\n831\t// Parameters:\n832\t//   - fields: Slice of field names\n833\t//\n834\t// Returns a formatted list string.\n835\t//\n836\t// Example usage:\n837\t//\n838\t//\tlist := FormatFieldList([]string{\"email\", \"password\"})\n839\t//\t// Returns: \"email and password\"\n840\tfunc FormatFieldList(fields []string) string {\n841\t\treturn FormatFieldListWith(fields, \"and\")\n842\t}\n843\t\n844\t// FormatValidationErrorToString is an alias for FormatValidationErrorFull.\n845\t// This provides a more explicit name for converting a ValidationError to a string.\n846\t//\n847\t// Parameters:\n848\t//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n849\t//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n850\t//\n851\t// Returns a formatted error message string.\n852\tfunc FormatValidationErrorToString(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n853\t\treturn FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity)\n854\t}\n855\t\n856\t// TruncateValue is an alias for TruncateString for compatibility.\n857\t//\n858\t// Parameters:\n859\t//   - value: The value to truncate\n860\t//   - maxLength: Maximum length (including ellipsis)\n861\t//\n862\t// Returns a truncated value string with ellipsis if needed.\n863\tfunc TruncateValue(value string, maxLength int) string {\n864\t\treturn TruncateString(value, maxLength)\n865\t}\n866\t\n867\t// =============================================================================\n868\t// HELPER FUNCTIONS\n869\t// =============================================================================\n870\t\n871\t// QuoteValue wraps a value in quotes for display in error messages.\n872\t// Empty values return \"(empty)\" instead of quotes.\n873\t//\n874\t// Parameters:\n875\t//   - value: The value to quote\n876\t//\n877\t// Returns a quoted value string.\n878\t//\n879\t// Example usage:\n880\t//\n881\t//\tquoted := QuoteValue(\"email\")\n882\t//\t// Returns: \"'email'\"\n883\t//\n884\t//\tquoted := QuoteValue(\"\")\n885\t//\t// Returns: \"(empty)\"\n886\tfunc QuoteValue(value string) string {\n887\t\tif value == \"\" {\n888\t\t\treturn \"(empty)\"\n889\t\t}\n890\t\n891\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", value)\n892\t}\n893\t\n894\t// TruncateString truncates a string value to a maximum length for display.\n895\t// This is useful for keeping error messages concise.\n896\t// Unlike TruncateValue in format_helpers.go, this takes a string and maxLength.\n897\t//\n898\t// Parameters:\n899\t//   - value: The value to truncate\n900\t//   - maxLength: Maximum length (including ellipsis)\n901\t//\n902\t// Returns a truncated value string with ellipsis if needed.\n903\t//\n904\t// Example usage:\n905\t//\n906\t//\ttruncated := TruncateString(\"very long string here\", 10)\n907\t//\t// Returns: \"very long...\"\n908\tfunc TruncateString(value string, maxLength int) string {\n909\t\tif maxLength < 3 {\n910\t\t\tmaxLength = 3\n911\t\t}\n912\t\n913\t\tif len(value) <= maxLength {\n914\t\t\treturn value\n915\t\t}\n916\t\n917\t\treturn value[:maxLength-3] + \"...\"\n918\t}\n919\t\n920\t// =============================================================================\n921\t// ADDITIONAL ERRORTYPE ENUM INTEGRATION\n922\t// =============================================================================\n923\t\n924\t// FormatErrorWithType formats an error message using the ErrorType enum.\n925\t// This provides type-safe error formatting and automatic severity detection.\n926\t//\n927\t// Parameters:\n928\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat)\n929\t//   - message: The error message content\n930\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n931\t//\n932\t// Returns a formatted error message with automatic error type conversion.\n933\t//\n934\t// Example usage:\n935\t//\n936\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithType(ErrTypeRequired, \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n937\t//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: Field is required\"\n938\t//\n939\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithType(ErrTypeFormat, \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n940\t//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\n941\tfunc FormatErrorWithType(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n942\t\terrorTypeStr := errorType.String()\n943\t\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorTypeStr, message, fieldName)\n944\t}\n945\t\n946\t// FormatErrorWithTypeAndSeverity formats an error message with explicit severity.\n947\t// This combines ErrorType enum with custom severity levels for enhanced error display.\n948\t//\n949\t// Parameters:\n950\t//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n951\t//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity level to use\n952\t//   - message: The error message content\n953\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n954\t//\n955\t// Returns a formatted error message with severity indicator.\n956\t//\n957\t// Example usage:\n958\t//\n959\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithTypeAndSeverity(ErrTypeRequired, SeverityHigh, \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n960\t//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] High [required] email: Field is required\"\n961\tfunc FormatErrorWithTypeAndSeverity(errorType ErrorType, severity ErrorSeverity, message string, fieldName string) string {\n962\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n963\t\n964\t\t// Add severity indicator\n965\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)))\n966\t\n967\t\t// Add error type, field, and message\n968\t\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorWithType(errorType, message, fieldName))\n969\t\n970\t\treturn builder.String()\n971\t}\n972\t\n973\t// FormatErrorWithCategory formats an error message using ErrorCategory.\n974\t// This provides category-aware error formatting for better error organization.\n975\t//\n976\t// Parameters:\n977\t//   - category: The ErrorCategory value\n978\t//   - message: The error message content\n979\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n980\t//\n981\t// Returns a formatted error message with category label.\n982\t//\n983\t// Example usage:\n984\t//\n985\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithCategory(CategoryHTTP, \"Invalid status code\", \"response.status\")\n986\t//\t// Returns: \"[HTTP Protocol] response.status: Invalid status code\"\n987\tfunc FormatErrorWithCategory(category ErrorCategory, message string, fieldName string) string {\n988\t\tcategoryLabel := FormatCategory(category)\n989\t\treturn FormatErrorMessage(categoryLabel, message, fieldName)\n990\t}\n991\t\n992\t// FormatErrorWithCategoryExplicit formats an error message with category and explicit severity.\n993\t// This provides comprehensive error formatting with both category classification and severity.\n994\t// Note: This differs from FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity by taking explicit category and severity.\n995\t//\n996\t// Parameters:\n997\t//   - category: The ErrorCategory value\n998\t//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity level to use\n999\t//   - message: The error message content\n1000\t//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n1001\t//\n1002\t// Returns a formatted error message with category and severity.\n1003\t//\n1004\t// Example usage:\n1005\t//\n1006\t//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithCategoryExplicit(CategoryValidation, SeverityHigh, \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n1007\t//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] High [Data Validation] email: Field is required\"\n1008\tfunc FormatErrorWithCategoryExplicit(category ErrorCategory, severity ErrorSeverity, message string, fieldName string) string {\n1009\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n1010\t\n1011\t\t// Add severity indicator\n1012\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)))\n1013\t\n1014\t\t// Add category and message\n1015\t\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorWithCategory(category, message, fieldName))\n1016\t\n1017\t\treturn builder.String()\n1018\t}\n1019\t\n1020\t// =============================================================================\n1021\t// FIELD REFERENCE FORMATTING WITH EXPLICIT CATEGORY\n1022\t// required\"\n1097\tfunc FormatValidationErrorWithTypeOverride(err ValidationError, errorType ErrorType, includeSeverity bool) string {\n1098\t\tvar builder strings.Builder\n1099\t\n1100\t\t// Add []ValidationError{{\n1174\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n1175\t//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n1176\t//\t}, {\n1177\t//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeFormat),\n1178\t//\t    FieldName: FormatErrorListBySeverity(errors []ValidationError, minSeverity ErrorSeverity) string {\n1184\t\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n1185\t\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n1186\t\t}\n1187\t\n1188\t\t// Filter by minimum severity manually\n1189\t\tvar filtered []ValidationError\n1190\t\tfor _, err := range errors {\n1191\t\t\t// Try to get severity from ErrorType enum first\n1192\t\t\terrorType := ErrorTypeFromString(err.ErrorType)\n1193\t\t\tvar errSeverity ErrorSeverity\n1194\t\t\tif errorType.IsValid() && errorType != ErrTypeUnknown {\n1195\t\t\t\terrSeverity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n1196\t\t\t} else {\n1197\t\t\t\t// Fall back to string-based error type lookup\n1198\t\t\t\terrSeverity = GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(err.ErrorType)\n1199\t\t\t}\n1200\t\t\tif errSeverity.Compare(minSeverity) >= 0 {\n1201\t\t\t\tfiltered = append(filtered, err)\n1202\t\t\t}\n1203\t\t}\n1204\t\n1205\t\tif len(filtered) == 0 {\n1206\t\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"No errors at or above %s severity\", FormatSeverity(minSeverity))\n1207\t\t}\n1208\t\n1209\t\t// Group filtered errors by severity\n1210\t\tfilteredGroup := NewErrorSeverityGroup(filtered)\n1211\t\n1212\t\tvar lines []string\n1213\t\n1214\t\t// Process severities in order (critical to info)\n1215\t\tseverityOrder := []ErrorSeverity{SeverityCritical, SeverityHigh, SeverityMedium, SeverityLow, SeverityInfo}\n1216\t\n1217\t\tfor _, severity := range severityOrder {\n1218\t\t\tsevErrors := filteredGroup.GetErrors(severity)\n1219\t\t\tif len(sevErrors) == 0 {\n1220\t\t\t\tcontinue\n1221\t\t\t}\n1222\t\n1223\t\t\t// Add severity header\n1224\t\t\tlines = {\n1228\t\t\t\tlines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf(\"  - %s\", FormatValidationErrorWithAutoType(err, false)))\n1229\t\t\t}\n1230\t\t}\n1231\t\n1232\t\treturn strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n1233\t}\n1234\t\n1235\t// FormatErrorListByCategory formats multiple errors grouped by category.\n1236\t// This provides a category-organized view of validation failures.\n1237\t//\n1238\t// Content, Validation, etc.)\n1255\tfunc FormatErrorListByCategory(errors []ValidationError) string {\n1256\t\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n1257\t\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n1258\t\t}\n1259\t\n1260\t\t// Create category group\n1261\t\tgroup := NewErrorCategoryGroup(errors)\n1262\t\n1263\t\tvar lines []string\n1264\t\n1265\t\t// Get all categories with errors\n1266\t\tcategories :=
