{\n1271\t\t\t\tcontinue\n1272\t\t\t}\n1273\t\n1274\t\t\t// Add category header\n1275\t\t\tcategoryLabel := FormatCategory(category)\n1276\t\t\tlines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%d errors):\", categoryLabel, len(catErrors)))\n1277\t\n1278\t\t\t// Add errors under this category\n1279\t\t\tfor _, err := range catErrors {\n1280\t\t\t\tlines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf(\"  - %s\", FormatErrorMessage(err.ErrorType, err.Message, err.FieldName)))\n1281\t\t\t}\n1282\t\t}\n1283\t\n1284\t\treturn strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n1285\t}\n1286\t"}]},"parent_tool_use_id":null,"session_id":"3c42a015-afad-459e-a97e-ab5c0cdb5bae","uuid":"a7704540-7693-49a1-a294-4cb0c8c2c4b7","timestamp":"2026-07-14T14:14:56.717Z","tool_use_result":{"type":"text","file":{"filePath":"/home/coding/ARMOR/internal/validate/error_formatting.go","content":"package validate\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n/*\nError Formatting Helpers\n\nThis file provides helper functions for formatting validation errors consistently.\nThese helpers work with the ErrorType enum, ErrorCategory, and ErrorSeverity types\nto produce human-readable, well-formatted error messages.\n*/\n\n// =============================================================================\n// FIELD REFERENCE FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatFieldRef creates a standardized field reference string with optional parent.\n// This ensures field names are consistently formatted across error messages.\n// Supports nested field paths and array index formatting.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The name of the field (e.g., \"email\", \"user.address\", \"users.0.email\")\n//   - parent: Optional parent path (e.g., \"request\", \"response.body\")\n//\n// Returns a formatted field reference string with array indices in bracket notation.\n// Empty or invalid paths return \"(unknown field)\" for consistent error display.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"email\", \"request\")\n//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"email\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"users.0.email\", \"response\")\n//\t// Returns: \"response.users[0].email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldRef(\"\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"(unknown field)\"\nfunc FormatFieldRef(fieldName string, parent string) string {\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\tparent = strings.TrimSpace(parent)\n\n\tif fieldName == \"\" {\n\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n\t\t\t// Normalize parent path even when field is empty\n\t\t\tnormalizedParent := FormatFieldPath(parent)\n\t\t\tif normalizedParent != \"(unknown field)\" {\n\t\t\t\treturn normalizedParent\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t\treturn parent\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Normalize field name to handle array indices (e.g., \"users.0.email\" → \"users[0].email\")\n\tnormalizedFieldName := FormatFieldPath(fieldName)\n\n\t// Check if normalization resulted in unknown field indicator\n\tif normalizedFieldName == \"(unknown field)\" {\n\t\tif parent != \"\" {\n\t\t\treturn parent\n\t\t}\n\t\treturn \"(unknown field)\"\n\t}\n\n\tif parent != \"\" {\n\t\t// Normalize parent as well in case it contains array indices\n\t\tnormalizedParent := FormatFieldPath(parent)\n\n\t\t// If parent normalizes to unknown field, just return the field name\n\t\tif normalizedParent == \"(unknown field)\" {\n\t\t\treturn normalizedFieldName\n\t\t}\n\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s.%s\", normalizedParent, normalizedFieldName)\n\t}\n\n\treturn normalizedFieldName\n}\n\n// FormatFieldLocationWith creates a detailed field location string with context.\n// This is useful for error messages that need to show exactly where an error occurred.\n// Unlike FormatFieldLocation in format_helpers.go, this supports parent paths.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - location: Additional location context (e.g., \"line 5\", \"position 123\")\n//   - parent: Optional parent path\n//\n// Returns a formatted location string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tloc := FormatFieldLocationWith(\"email\", \"line 5\", \"request\")\n//\t// Returns: \"request.email at line 5\"\n//\n//\tloc := FormatFieldLocationWith(\"email\", \"\", \"request\")\n//\t// Returns: \"request.email\"\nfunc FormatFieldLocationWith(fieldName string, location string, parent string) string {\n\tref := FormatFieldRef(fieldName, parent)\n\tlocation = strings.TrimSpace(location)\n\n\tif location != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s at %s\", ref, location)\n\t}\n\n\treturn ref\n}\n\n// FormatFieldListWith formats a list of field names into a readable string with conjunction.\n// Unlike FormatFieldList in format_helpers.go, this supports custom conjunctions.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fields: Slice of field names\n//   - conjunction: The conjunction to use (e.g., \"and\", \"or\")\n//\n// Returns a formatted list string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlist := FormatFieldListWith([]string{\"email\", \"password\"}, \"and\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email and password\"\n//\n//\tlist := FormatFieldListWith([]string{\"email\", \"password\", \"name\"}, \"and\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email, password, and name\"\nfunc FormatFieldListWith(fields []string, conjunction string) string {\n\tif len(fields) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"\"\n\t}\n\n\tif len(fields) == 1 {\n\t\treturn fields[0]\n\t}\n\n\tif len(fields) == 2 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s %s %s\", fields[0], conjunction, fields[1])\n\t}\n\n\t// Oxford comma style\n\tallButLast := fields[:len(fields)-1]\n\tlast := fields[len(fields)-1]\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s, %s %s\", strings.Join(allButLast, \", \"), conjunction, last)\n}\n\n// FormatFieldReferenceWithType creates a field reference with ErrorType context.\n// This provides type-specific field reference formatting that includes\n// contextual information based on the error type.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - parent: Optional parent path\n//\n// Returns a formatted field reference string with error type context.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldReferenceWithType(ErrTypeRequired, \"email\", \"request\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[Required Field] request.email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldReferenceWithType(ErrTypeFormat, \"email\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[Format Error] email\"\nfunc FormatFieldReferenceWithType(errorType ErrorType, fieldName string, parent string) string {\n\tfieldRef := FormatFieldRef(fieldName, parent)\n\terrorTypeLabel := FormatErrorType(errorType)\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] %s\", errorTypeLabel, fieldRef)\n}\n\n// FormatFieldReferenceWithCategory creates a field reference with category context.\n// This formats field references with their error category for better context.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - parent: Optional parent path\n//\n// Returns a formatted field reference string with category context.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldReferenceWithCategory(ErrTypeRequired, \"email\", \"request\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[Data Validation] request.email\"\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldReferenceWithCategory(ErrTypeFormat, \"email\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[Data Validation] email\"\nfunc FormatFieldReferenceWithCategory(errorType ErrorType, fieldName string, parent string) string {\n\tfieldRef := FormatFieldRef(fieldName, parent)\n\tcategory := GetCategoryForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n\tcategoryLabel := FormatCategory(category)\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] %s\", categoryLabel, fieldRef)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR MESSAGE FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatErrorMessage creates a standardized error message from components.\n// This ensures error messages are consistently formatted.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The type of error (use ErrorType enum or string)\n//   - message: The core error message\n//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessage(\"required\", \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: Field is required\"\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessage(\"format\", \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\nfunc FormatErrorMessage(errorType string, message string, fieldName string) string {\n\terrorType = strings.TrimSpace(errorType)\n\tmessage = strings.TrimSpace(message)\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\n\tvar builder strings.Builder\n\n\t// Add error type in brackets\n\tif errorType != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", errorType))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add field name if present\n\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add message\n\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n\n\treturn builder.String()\n}\n\n// FormatErrorMessageWithType creates a standardized error message using ErrorType enum.\n// This provides type-safe error type specification and consistent formatting.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n//   - message: The core error message\n//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessageWithType(ErrTypeRequired, \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: Field is required\"\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorMessageWithType(ErrTypeFormat, \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\nfunc FormatErrorMessageWithType(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorType.String(), message, fieldName)\n}\n\n// FormatErrorWithSeverity creates an error message with severity information.\n// This integrates ErrorType enum with automatic severity lookup.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n//   - message: The core error message\n//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string with severity prefix.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithSeverity(ErrTypeRequired, \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] High [required] email: Field is required\"\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithSeverity(ErrTypeFormat, \"Invalid format\", \"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[■] Medium [format] email: Invalid format\"\nfunc FormatErrorWithSeverity(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n\tseverity := GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n\tseverityLabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)\n\terrorTypeLabel := errorType.String()\n\n\tvar builder strings.Builder\n\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s [%s] \", severityLabel, errorTypeLabel))\n\n\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n\t}\n\n\tbuilder.WriteString(message)\n\n\treturn builder.String()\n}\n\n// FormatErrorWithValues creates an error message showing expected vs actual values.\n// This is useful for validation errors where values don't match expectations.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - expected: The expected value\n//   - actual: The actual value\n//   - customMessage: Optional custom message prefix\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message with value comparison.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithValues(\"status_code\", 200, 404, \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"status_code: expected 200, got 404\"\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithValues(\"count\", 5, 3, \"Not enough items\")\n//\t// Returns: \"count: Not enough items (expected 5, got 3)\"\nfunc FormatErrorWithValues(fieldName string, expected interface{}, actual interface{}, customMessage string) string {\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\tcustomMessage = strings.TrimSpace(customMessage)\n\n\tvar builder strings.Builder\n\n\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: \", fieldName))\n\t}\n\n\tif customMessage != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", customMessage))\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"(expected %v, got %v)\", expected, actual))\n\t} else {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected %v, got %v\", expected, actual))\n\t}\n\n\treturn builder.String()\n}\n\n// FormatErrorWithRange creates an error message for range validation failures.\n// This is useful for numeric range validations or length validations.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - minValue: The minimum allowed value\n//   - maxValue: The maximum allowed value\n//   - actualValue: The actual value that failed validation\n//\n// Returns a formatted range error message.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithRange(\"age\", 0, 120, 150)\n//\t// Returns: \"age: value 150 is outside valid range [0, 120]\"\nfunc FormatErrorWithRange(fieldName string, minValue interface{}, maxValue interface{}, actualValue interface{}) string {\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\n\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: value %v is outside valid range [%v, %v]\",\n\t\t\tfieldName, actualValue, minValue, maxValue)\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"value %v is outside valid range [%v, %v]\",\n\t\tactualValue, minValue, maxValue)\n}\n\n// FormatErrorWithPattern creates an error message for pattern validation failures.\n// This is useful for format validations like email, URL, regex patterns.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - pattern: The pattern that failed (can be a description)\n//   - actualValue: The actual value that failed validation\n//\n// Returns a formatted pattern error message.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithPattern(\"email\", \"must contain @\", \"invalidemail\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email: value 'invalidemail' does not match pattern (must contain @)\"\nfunc FormatErrorWithPattern(fieldName string, pattern string, actualValue interface{}) string {\n\tfieldName = strings.TrimSpace(fieldName)\n\tpattern = strings.TrimSpace(pattern)\n\n\tif fieldName != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: value '%v' does not match pattern (%s)\",\n\t\t\tfieldName, actualValue, pattern)\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"value '%v' does not match pattern (%s)\",\n\t\tactualValue, pattern)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR TYPE FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatErrorType creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorType enum.\n// This converts technical error type names to readable labels.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n//\n// Returns a formatted error type label.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatErrorType(ErrTypeRequired)\n//\t// Returns: \"Required Field\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatErrorType(ErrTypeFormat)\n//\t// Returns: \"Format Error\"\nfunc FormatErrorType(errorType ErrorType) string {\n\tswitch errorType {\n\tcase ErrTypeRequired:\n\t\treturn \"Required Field\"\n\tcase ErrTypeFormat:\n\t\treturn \"Format Error\"\n\tcase ErrTypeRange:\n\t\treturn \"Range Error\"\n\tcase ErrTypeLength:\n\t\treturn \"Length Error\"\n\tcase ErrTypeType:\n\t\treturn \"Type Error\"\n\tcase ErrTypeValue:\n\t\treturn \"Invalid Value\"\n\tcase ErrTypeDuplicate:\n\t\treturn \"Duplicate Value\"\n\tcase ErrTypeConflict:\n\t\treturn \"Conflict\"\n\tcase ErrTypeUnknown:\n\t\treturn \"Unknown Error\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn string(errorType)\n\t}\n}\n\n// FormatErrorTypeFrom creates a user-friendly label from a string error type.\n// This converts technical error type strings to readable labels.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorTypeStr: The error type string\n//\n// Returns a formatted error type label.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatErrorTypeFrom(\"required\")\n//\t// Returns: \"Required Field\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatErrorTypeFrom(\"format\")\n//\t// Returns: \"Format Error\"\nfunc FormatErrorTypeFrom(errorTypeStr string) string {\n\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(errorTypeStr)\n\treturn FormatErrorType(et)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// CATEGORY FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatCategory creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorCategory.\n// This converts technical category names to readable labels.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - category: The ErrorCategory value\n//\n// Returns a formatted category label.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatCategory(CategoryHTTP)\n//\t// Returns: \"HTTP Protocol\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatCategory(CategoryValidation)\n//\t// Returns: \"Data Validation\"\nfunc FormatCategory(category ErrorCategory) string {\n\tswitch category {\n\tcase CategoryHTTP:\n\t\treturn \"HTTP Protocol\"\n\tcase CategoryContent:\n\t\treturn \"Content\"\n\tcase CategoryValidation:\n\t\treturn \"Data Validation\"\n\tcase CategoryPerformance:\n\t\treturn \"Performance\"\n\tcase CategorySecurity:\n\t\treturn \"Security\"\n\tcase CategoryCustom:\n\t\treturn \"Custom\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn string(category)\n\t}\n}\n\n// FormatErrorByCategory creates a category-aware error message.\n// This formats errors according to their category, including appropriate\n// contextual information based on the error type's category.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n//   - message: The core error message\n//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string with category context.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorByCategory(ErrTypeRequired, \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[Data Validation] [required] email: Field is required\"\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorByCategory(ErrTypeFormat, \"Invalid format\", \"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[Data Validation] [format] email: Invalid format\"\nfunc FormatErrorByCategory(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n\tcategory := GetCategoryForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n\tcategoryLabel := FormatCategory(category)\n\n\tvar builder strings.Builder\n\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] \", categoryLabel))\n\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorMessageWithType(errorType, message, fieldName))\n\n\treturn builder.String()\n}\n\n// FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity creates a comprehensive error message\n// with both category and severity information.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n//   - message: The core error message\n//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string with severity, category, and error type.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(ErrTypeRequired, \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] High [Data Validation] [required] email: Field is required\"\nfunc FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n\tseverity := GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n\tseverityLabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)\n\tcategory := GetCategoryForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n\tcategoryLabel := FormatCategory(category)\n\n\tvar builder strings.Builder\n\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s [%s] \", severityLabel, categoryLabel))\n\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorMessageWithType(errorType, message, fieldName))\n\n\treturn builder.String()\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// SEVERITY FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatSeverity creates a user-friendly label from an ErrorSeverity.\n// This converts technical severity names to readable labels.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity value\n//\n// Returns a formatted severity label.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatSeverity(SeverityCritical)\n//\t// Returns: \"CRITICAL\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatSeverity(SeverityHigh)\n//\t// Returns: \"High\"\nfunc FormatSeverity(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n\tswitch severity {\n\tcase SeverityCritical:\n\t\treturn \"CRITICAL\"\n\tcase SeverityHigh:\n\t\treturn \"High\"\n\tcase SeverityMedium:\n\t\treturn \"Medium\"\n\tcase SeverityLow:\n\t\treturn \"Low\"\n\tcase SeverityInfo:\n\t\treturn \"Info\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn string(severity)\n\t}\n}\n\n// FormatSeverityWithIndicator adds visual indicators to severity labels.\n// This is useful for console output or UI display.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity value\n//\n// Returns a severity label with visual indicator.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(SeverityCritical)\n//\t// Returns: \"[!] CRITICAL\"\n//\n//\tlabel := FormatSeverityWithIndicator(SeverityHigh)\n//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] High\"\nfunc FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n\tindicator := severityIndicator(severity)\n\tlabel := FormatSeverity(severity)\n\n\tif indicator != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"[%s] %s\", indicator, label)\n\t}\n\n\treturn label\n}\n\n// severityIndicator returns a visual indicator for a severity level.\nfunc severityIndicator(severity ErrorSeverity) string {\n\tswitch severity {\n\tcase SeverityCritical:\n\t\treturn \"!\"\n\tcase SeverityHigh:\n\t\treturn \"⚠\"\n\tcase SeverityMedium:\n\t\treturn \"■\"\n\tcase SeverityLow:\n\t\treturn \"○\"\n\tcase SeverityInfo:\n\t\treturn \"i\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn \"?\"\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// COMPREHENSIVE ERROR FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatValidationErrorFull creates a comprehensive, formatted error message from a ValidationError.\n// This combines all relevant error information into a single, readable message.\n// Unlike FormatValidationError in format_helpers.go, this takes a ValidationError struct.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := ValidationError{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, true)\n//\t// Returns: \"[High] [required] email: Field is required\"\nfunc FormatValidationErrorFull(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n\tvar builder strings.Builder\n\n\t// Add severity if requested\n\tif includeSeverity {\n\t\t// Try to get severity from ErrorType enum first\n\t\tet := ErrorTypeFromString(err.ErrorType)\n\t\tvar severity ErrorSeverity\n\t\tif et.IsValid() && et != ErrTypeUnknown {\n\t\t\tseverity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(et)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// Fall back to string-based error type lookup\n\t\t\tseverity = GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(err.ErrorType)\n\t\t}\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)))\n\t}\n\n\t// Add error type and field\n\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorMessage(err.ErrorType, err.Message, err.FieldName))\n\n\t// Add expected/actual values if present\n\tif err.Expected != nil || err.Actual != nil {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n\t\tif err.Expected != nil {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %v\", err.Expected))\n\t\t}\n\t\tif err.Expected != nil && err.Actual != nil {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\", \")\n\t\t}\n\t\tif err.Actual != nil {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %v\", err.Actual))\n\t\t}\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Add location if present\n\tif err.Location != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" [%s]\", err.Location))\n\t}\n\n\treturn builder.String()\n}\n\n// FormatValidationErrorBrief creates a brief, one-line error message.\n// This is useful for summaries and compact displays.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n//\n// Returns a brief error message string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := ValidationError{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorBrief(err)\n//\t// Returns: \"email: required\"\nfunc FormatValidationErrorBrief(err ValidationError) string {\n\tif err.FieldName != \"\" {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %s\", err.FieldName, err.ErrorType)\n\t}\n\n\treturn err.ErrorType\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR LIST FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatErrorList formats multiple errors into a readable list.\n// This is useful for displaying multiple validation failures.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to format\n//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n//\n// Returns a formatted error list string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}, {\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeFormat),\n//\t    Message: \"Invalid format\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n//\t}}\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorList(errors, true)\n//\t// Returns:\n//\t// 1. [High] [required] email: Field is required\n//\t// 2. [High] [format] password: Invalid format\nfunc FormatErrorList(errors []ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n\t}\n\n\tvar lines []string\n\tfor i, err := range errors {\n\t\tprefix := fmt.Sprintf(\"%d. \", i+1)\n\t\tformatted := FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity)\n\t\tlines = append(lines, prefix+formatted)\n\t}\n\n\treturn strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n}\n\n// FormatErrorListSummary creates a summary of multiple errors.\n// This provides a high-level overview of validation failures.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to summarize\n//\n// Returns a summary string with error counts by type.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}, {\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n//\t}}\n//\n//\tsummary := FormatErrorListSummary(errors)\n//\t// Returns: \"2 errors: required (2)\"\nfunc FormatErrorListSummary(errors []ValidationError) string {\n\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Count errors by type\n\tcounts := make(map[string]int)\n\tfor _, err := range errors {\n\t\tcounts[err.ErrorType]++\n\t}\n\n\t// Build summary parts\n\tvar parts []string\n\tfor errorType, count := range counts {\n\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%d)\", errorType, count))\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%d error(s): %s\", len(errors), strings.Join(parts, \", \"))\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// CONVENIENCE WRAPPER FUNCTIONS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatFieldLocation is a convenience wrapper for FormatFieldLocationWith\n// without the parent parameter.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - location: Additional location context\n//\n// Returns a formatted location string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tloc := FormatFieldLocation(\"email\", \"line 5\")\n//\t// Returns: \"email at line 5\"\nfunc FormatFieldLocation(fieldName string, location string) string {\n\treturn FormatFieldLocationWith(fieldName, location, \"\")\n}\n\n// FormatFieldList is a convenience wrapper for FormatFieldListWith\n// with \"and\" as the default conjunction.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fields: Slice of field names\n//\n// Returns a formatted list string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tlist := FormatFieldList([]string{\"email\", \"password\"})\n//\t// Returns: \"email and password\"\nfunc FormatFieldList(fields []string) string {\n\treturn FormatFieldListWith(fields, \"and\")\n}\n\n// FormatValidationErrorToString is an alias for FormatValidationErrorFull.\n// This provides a more explicit name for converting a ValidationError to a string.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message string.\nfunc FormatValidationErrorToString(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n\treturn FormatValidationErrorFull(err, includeSeverity)\n}\n\n// TruncateValue is an alias for TruncateString for compatibility.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - value: The value to truncate\n//   - maxLength: Maximum length (including ellipsis)\n//\n// Returns a truncated value string with ellipsis if needed.\nfunc TruncateValue(value string, maxLength int) string {\n\treturn TruncateString(value, maxLength)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// HELPER FUNCTIONS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// QuoteValue wraps a value in quotes for display in error messages.\n// Empty values return \"(empty)\" instead of quotes.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - value: The value to quote\n//\n// Returns a quoted value string.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tquoted := QuoteValue(\"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"'email'\"\n//\n//\tquoted := QuoteValue(\"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"(empty)\"\nfunc QuoteValue(value string) string {\n\tif value == \"\" {\n\t\treturn \"(empty)\"\n\t}\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"'%s'\", value)\n}\n\n// TruncateString truncates a string value to a maximum length for display.\n// This is useful for keeping error messages concise.\n// Unlike TruncateValue in format_helpers.go, this takes a string and maxLength.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - value: The value to truncate\n//   - maxLength: Maximum length (including ellipsis)\n//\n// Returns a truncated value string with ellipsis if needed.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\ttruncated := TruncateString(\"very long string here\", 10)\n//\t// Returns: \"very long...\"\nfunc TruncateString(value string, maxLength int) string {\n\tif maxLength < 3 {\n\t\tmaxLength = 3\n\t}\n\n\tif len(value) <= maxLength {\n\t\treturn value\n\t}\n\n\treturn value[:maxLength-3] + \"...\"\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ADDITIONAL ERRORTYPE ENUM INTEGRATION\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatErrorWithType formats an error message using the ErrorType enum.\n// This provides type-safe error formatting and automatic severity detection.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat)\n//   - message: The error message content\n//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message with automatic error type conversion.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithType(ErrTypeRequired, \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[required] email: Field is required\"\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithType(ErrTypeFormat, \"Invalid email format\", \"\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[format] Invalid email format\"\nfunc FormatErrorWithType(errorType ErrorType, message string, fieldName string) string {\n\terrorTypeStr := errorType.String()\n\treturn FormatErrorMessage(errorTypeStr, message, fieldName)\n}\n\n// FormatErrorWithTypeAndSeverity formats an error message with explicit severity.\n// This combines ErrorType enum with custom severity levels for enhanced error display.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value\n//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity level to use\n//   - message: The error message content\n//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message with severity indicator.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithTypeAndSeverity(ErrTypeRequired, SeverityHigh, \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] High [required] email: Field is required\"\nfunc FormatErrorWithTypeAndSeverity(errorType ErrorType, severity ErrorSeverity, message string, fieldName string) string {\n\tvar builder strings.Builder\n\n\t// Add severity indicator\n\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)))\n\n\t// Add error type, field, and message\n\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorWithType(errorType, message, fieldName))\n\n\treturn builder.String()\n}\n\n// FormatErrorWithCategory formats an error message using ErrorCategory.\n// This provides category-aware error formatting for better error organization.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - category: The ErrorCategory value\n//   - message: The error message content\n//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message with category label.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithCategory(CategoryHTTP, \"Invalid status code\", \"response.status\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[HTTP Protocol] response.status: Invalid status code\"\nfunc FormatErrorWithCategory(category ErrorCategory, message string, fieldName string) string {\n\tcategoryLabel := FormatCategory(category)\n\treturn FormatErrorMessage(categoryLabel, message, fieldName)\n}\n\n// FormatErrorWithCategoryExplicit formats an error message with category and explicit severity.\n// This provides comprehensive error formatting with both category classification and severity.\n// Note: This differs from FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity by taking explicit category and severity.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - category: The ErrorCategory value\n//   - severity: The ErrorSeverity level to use\n//   - message: The error message content\n//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message with category and severity.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithCategoryExplicit(CategoryValidation, SeverityHigh, \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] High [Data Validation] email: Field is required\"\nfunc FormatErrorWithCategoryExplicit(category ErrorCategory, severity ErrorSeverity, message string, fieldName string) string {\n\tvar builder strings.Builder\n\n\t// Add severity indicator\n\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)))\n\n\t// Add category and message\n\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorWithCategory(category, message, fieldName))\n\n\treturn builder.String()\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// FIELD REFERENCE FORMATTING WITH EXPLICIT CATEGORY\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatFieldReferenceWithExplicitCategory formats a field reference with explicit ErrorCategory context.\n// This provides category-aware field reference formatting.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - fieldName: The field name\n//   - category: The ErrorCategory value\n//   - parent: Optional parent path\n//\n// Returns a formatted field reference with category context.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tref := FormatFieldReferenceWithExplicitCategory(\"status_code\", CategoryHTTP, \"response\")\n//\t// Returns: \"response.status_code (HTTP Protocol)\"\nfunc FormatFieldReferenceWithExplicitCategory(fieldName string, category ErrorCategory, parent string) string {\n\tref := FormatFieldRef(fieldName, parent)\n\tcategoryLabel := FormatCategory(category)\n\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%s)\", ref, categoryLabel)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// TYPE DETENTION AND AUTO-FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatErrorWithTypeDetection automatically detects ErrorType from string and formats.\n// This bridges the gap between string-based error types and the ErrorType enum.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errorTypeStr: The error type string (e.g., \"required\", \"format\")\n//   - message: The error message content\n//   - fieldName: Optional field name where the error occurred\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message with detected type and automatic severity.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithTypeDetection(\"required\", \"Field is required\", \"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] High [required] email: Field is required\"\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorWithTypeDetection(\"format\", \"Invalid format\", \"email\")\n//\t// Returns: \"[■] Medium [format] email: Invalid format\"\nfunc FormatErrorWithTypeDetection(errorTypeStr string, message string, fieldName string) string {\n\terrorType := ErrorTypeFromString(errorTypeStr)\n\n\t// Use the detected type for category-aware formatting\n\treturn FormatErrorWithCategoryAndSeverity(errorType, message, fieldName)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ENHANCED VALIDATION ERROR FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatValidationErrorWithType creates a comprehensive error message using explicit ErrorType enum.\n// This is the enhanced version that allows overriding the error type.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n//   - errorType: The ErrorType enum value (overrides err.ErrorType)\n//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message with full error details.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := ValidationError{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorWithType(err, ErrTypeRequired, true)\n//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] High [required] email: Field is required\"\nfunc FormatValidationErrorWithTypeOverride(err ValidationError, errorType ErrorType, includeSeverity bool) string {\n\tvar builder strings.Builder\n\n\t// Add severity if requested\n\tif includeSeverity {\n\t\t// Use the provided errorType for severity lookup\n\t\tseverity := GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"%s \", FormatSeverityWithIndicator(severity)))\n\t}\n\n\t// Use the provided errorType instead of err.ErrorType\n\terrorTypeStr := errorType.String()\n\tbuilder.WriteString(FormatErrorMessage(errorTypeStr, err.Message, err.FieldName))\n\n\t// Add expected/actual values if present\n\tif err.Expected != nil || err.Actual != nil {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\" (\")\n\t\tif err.Expected != nil {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"expected: %v\", err.Expected))\n\t\t}\n\t\tif err.Expected != nil && err.Actual != nil {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\", \")\n\t\t}\n\t\tif err.Actual != nil {\n\t\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\"actual: %v\", err.Actual))\n\t\t}\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(\")\")\n\t}\n\n\t// Add location if present\n\tif err.Location != \"\" {\n\t\tbuilder.WriteString(fmt.Sprintf(\" [%s]\", err.Location))\n\t}\n\n\treturn builder.String()\n}\n\n// FormatValidationErrorWithAutoType creates an error message with automatic ErrorType detection.\n// This automatically converts the ValidationError's string error type to ErrorType enum.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - err: The ValidationError to format\n//   - includeSeverity: Whether to include severity information\n//\n// Returns a formatted error message with auto-detected type and severity.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terr := ValidationError{\n//\t    ErrorType: \"required\",\n//\t    Message: \"Field is required\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatValidationErrorWithAutoType(err, true)\n//\t// Returns: \"[⚠] High [required] email: Field is required\"\nfunc FormatValidationErrorWithAutoType(err ValidationError, includeSeverity bool) string {\n\terrorType := ErrorTypeFromString(err.ErrorType)\n\treturn FormatValidationErrorWithTypeOverride(err, errorType, includeSeverity)\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// SEVERITY-AWARE ERROR LIST FORMATTING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// FormatErrorListBySeverity formats multiple errors grouped by severity level.\n// This provides a severity-organized view of validation failures.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to format\n//   - minSeverity: Minimum severity level to include (use SeverityInfo for all)\n//\n// Returns a formatted error list grouped by severity.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}, {\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeFormat),\n//\t    FieldName: \"password\",\n//\t}}\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorListBySeverity(errors, SeverityMedium)\n//\t// Returns errors grouped by severity, starting from Medium\nfunc FormatErrorListBySeverity(errors []ValidationError, minSeverity ErrorSeverity) string {\n\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Filter by minimum severity manually\n\tvar filtered []ValidationError\n\tfor _, err := range errors {\n\t\t// Try to get severity from ErrorType enum first\n\t\terrorType := ErrorTypeFromString(err.ErrorType)\n\t\tvar errSeverity ErrorSeverity\n\t\tif errorType.IsValid() && errorType != ErrTypeUnknown {\n\t\t\terrSeverity = GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(errorType)\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\t// Fall back to string-based error type lookup\n\t\t\terrSeverity = GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(err.ErrorType)\n\t\t}\n\t\tif errSeverity.Compare(minSeverity) >= 0 {\n\t\t\tfiltered = append(filtered, err)\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\tif len(filtered) == 0 {\n\t\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"No errors at or above %s severity\", FormatSeverity(minSeverity))\n\t}\n\n\t// Group filtered errors by severity\n\tfilteredGroup := NewErrorSeverityGroup(filtered)\n\n\tvar lines []string\n\n\t// Process severities in order (critical to info)\n\tseverityOrder := []ErrorSeverity{SeverityCritical, SeverityHigh, SeverityMedium, SeverityLow, SeverityInfo}\n\n\tfor _, severity := range severityOrder {\n\t\tsevErrors := filteredGroup.GetErrors(severity)\n\t\tif len(sevErrors) == 0 {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Add severity header\n\t\tlines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%d errors):\", FormatSeverity(severity), len(sevErrors)))\n\n\t\t// Add errors under this severity\n\t\tfor _, err := range sevErrors {\n\t\t\tlines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf(\"  - %s\", FormatValidationErrorWithAutoType(err, false)))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n}\n\n// FormatErrorListByCategory formats multiple errors grouped by category.\n// This provides a category-organized view of validation failures.\n//\n// Parameters:\n//   - errors: Slice of ValidationErrors to format\n//\n// Returns a formatted error list grouped by category.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\terrors := []ValidationError{{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}, {\n//\t    ErrorType: \"status_code\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"response.status\",\n//\t}}\n//\n//\tmsg := FormatErrorListByCategory(errors)\n//\t// Returns errors grouped by category (HTTP, Content, Validation, etc.)\nfunc FormatErrorListByCategory(errors []ValidationError) string {\n\tif len(errors) == 0 {\n\t\treturn \"No errors\"\n\t}\n\n\t// Create category group\n\tgroup := NewErrorCategoryGroup(errors)\n\n\tvar lines []string\n\n\t// Get all categories with errors\n\tcategories := group.GetCategories()\n\n\tfor _, category := range categories {\n\t\tcatErrors := group.GetErrors(category)\n\t\tif len(catErrors) == 0 {\n\t\t\tcontinue\n\t\t}\n\n\t\t// Add category header\n\t\tcategoryLabel := FormatCategory(category)\n\t\tlines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s (%d errors):\", categoryLabel, len(catErrors)))\n\n\t\t// Add errors under this category\n\t\tfor _, err := range catErrors {\n\t\t\tlines = append(lines, fmt.Sprintf(\"  - %s\", FormatErrorMessage(err.ErrorType, err.Message, err.FieldName)))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\n\treturn strings.Join(lines, \"\\n\")\n}\n","numLines":1286,"startLine":1,"totalLines":1286}}}

3,\n107\t\t\tSeverityCritical: 4,\n108\t\t}\n109\t\tdiff := severityOrder[es] - severityOrder[other]\n110\t\tif diff > 0 {\n111\t\t\treturn 1\n112\t\t} else if diff < 0 {\n113\t\t\treturn Headers\n161\t\tErrorTypeCORSHeaders:         SeverityMedium,\n162\t\tErrorTypeAuthHeaders:         SeverityCritical,\n163\t\tErrorTypeCustomHeaders:       SeverityLow,\n164\t\n165\t\t// Schema and data validation\n166\t\tErrorTypeJSONSchema:          SeverityHigh,\n167\t\tErrorTypeDataValidation:      SeverityHigh,\n168\t\tErrorTypeFieldValidation:     SeverityMedium,\n169\t\tErrorTypeTypeValidation:      SeverityHigh,\n170\t\n171\t\t// Performance\n172\t\tErrorTypeTimeout:             SeverityHigh,\n173\t\tErrorTypeRateLimit:           SeverityMedium,\n174\t\tErrorTypeRetryExceeded:       SeverityHigh,\n175\t\n176\t\t// Custom\n177\t\tErrorTypeCustom:              SeverityMedium,\n178\t\tErrorTypeUnknown:             SeverityLow,\n179\t}\n180\t\n181\t// GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType returns the default severity for a given error type.\n182\t// Returns SeverityLow if the error type is not recognized.\n183\tfunc GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(errorType string) ErrorSeverity {\n184\t\tif severity, ok := defaultSeverityForErrorType[errorType]; ok {\n185\t\t\treturn severity\n186\t\t}\n187\t\treturn SeverityLow\n188\t}\n189\t\n190\t// =============================================================================\n191\t// ERRORTYPE ENUM SEVERITY MAPPINGS\n192\t// =============================================================================\n193\t\n194\t// defaultSeverityForErrorTypeEnum defines the default severity for ErrorType enum values.\n195\t// This mapping is for the ErrorType enum from error_type.go (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat).\n196\tvar defaultSeverityForErrorTypeEnum = map[ErrorType]ErrorSeverity{\n197\t\t// Structural errors (missing fields, wrong types)\n198\t\tErrTypeRequired: SeverityHigh,      // Required field is missing\n199\t\tErrTypeType:     SeverityHigh,      // Wrong type (e.g., string instead of int)\n200\t\tErrTypeLength:   SeverityMedium,    // String/collection length issue\n201\t\n202\t\t// Semantic errors (format, range, value)\n203\t\tErrTypeFormat: SeverityMedium,      // Format doesn't match pattern (e.g., email)\n204\t\tErrTypeRange:  SeverityMedium,      // Value outside numeric range\n205\t\tErrTypeValue:  SeverityLow,         // Value invalid for domain-specific reasons\n206\t\n207\t\t// Constraint errors\n208\t\tErrTypeDuplicate: SeverityHigh,     // Duplicate value (uniqueness constraint)\n209\t\tErrTypeConflict:  SeverityMedium,   // Conflict with other values/constraints\n210\t\n211\t\t// Fallback\n212\t\tErrTypeUnknown: SeverityLow,        // Unknown error type\n213\t}\n214\t\n215\t// GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum returns the default severity for a given ErrorType enum value.\n216\t// Returns SeverityLow if the error type is not recognized.\n217\t//\n218\t// Example usage:\n219\t//\n220\t//\tseverity := GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(ErrTypeRequired)\n221\t//\t// Returns: SeverityHigh\n222\tfunc GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(errorType ErrorType) ErrorSeverity {\n223\t\tif severity, ok := validate\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR SEVERITY ENUM\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ErrorSeverity represents the severity level of a validation error.\n// Severity levels help prioritize error handling and user communication.\ntype ErrorSeverity string\n\nconst (\n\t// SeverityCritical indicates a critical error that prevents the system from functioning.\n\t// These errors typically indicate complete failure and require immediate attention.\n\t// Examples: Authentication failures, critical service unavailability.\n\tSeverityCritical ErrorSeverity = \"critical\"\n\n\t// SeverityHigh indicates a high-severity error that significantly impacts functionality.\n\t// These errors prevent core features from working but may have workarounds.\n\t// Examples: Missing required data, invalid authentication tokens.\n\tSeverityHigh ErrorSeverity = \"high\"\n\n\t// SeverityMedium indicates a medium-severity error that partially impacts functionality.\n\t// These errors may affect non-critical features or have mitigations.\n\t// Examples: Optional fields missing, non-critical validation failures.\n\tSeverityMedium ErrorSeverity = \"medium\"\n\n\t// SeverityLow indicates a low-severity error with minimal impact.\n\t// These errors typically represent minor issues or deviations.\n\t// Examples: Formatting issues, minor data inconsistencies.\n\tSeverityLow ErrorSeverity = \"low\"\n\n\t// SeverityInfo indicates an informational message that doesn't represent a failure.\n\t// These are typically warnings or notices for attention.\n\t// Examples: Deprecation notices, informational messages.\n\tSeverityInfo ErrorSeverity = \"info\"\n)\n\n// String returns the string representation of the ErrorSeverity.\nfunc (es ErrorSeverity) String() string {\n\treturn string(es)\n}\n\n// IsValid returns true if this is a valid ErrorSeverity constant.\nfunc (es ErrorSeverity) IsValid() bool {\n\tswitch es {\n\tcase SeverityCritical, SeverityHigh, SeverityMedium, SeverityLow, SeverityInfo:\n\t\treturn true\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n}\n\n// Description returns a human-readable description of this severity level.\nfunc (es ErrorSeverity) Description() string {\n\tswitch es {\n\tcase SeverityCritical:\n\t\treturn \"Critical error that prevents system functionality\"\n\tcase SeverityHigh:\n\t\treturn \"High-severity error that significantly impacts functionality\"\n\tcase SeverityMedium:\n\t\treturn \"Medium-severity error that partially impacts functionality\"\n\tcase SeverityLow:\n\t\treturn \"Low-severity error with minimal impact\"\n\tcase SeverityInfo:\n\t\treturn \"Informational message that doesn't represent a failure\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn \"Unknown severity level\"\n\t}\n}\n\n// IsCritical returns true if this is SeverityCritical.\nfunc (es ErrorSeverity) IsCritical() bool {\n\treturn es == SeverityCritical\n}\n\n// IsHigh returns true if this is SeverityHigh or higher.\nfunc (es ErrorSeverity) IsHigh() bool {\n\treturn es == SeverityHigh || es == SeverityCritical\n}\n\n// IsMediumOrHigher returns true if this is SeverityMedium or higher.\nfunc (es ErrorSeverity) IsMediumOrHigher() bool {\n\treturn es == SeverityMedium || es == SeverityHigh || es == SeverityCritical\n}\n\n// IsLowOrHigher returns true if this is SeverityLow or higher (everything except Info).\nfunc (es ErrorSeverity) IsLowOrHigher() bool {\n\treturn es == SeverityLow || es == SeverityMedium || es == SeverityHigh || es == SeverityCritical\n}\n\n// Compare compares this severity to another severity.\n// Returns:\n//   - positive if this severity is higher\n//   - negative if this severity is lower\n//   - 0 if they are equal\nfunc (es ErrorSeverity) Compare(other ErrorSeverity) int {\n\tseverityOrder := map[ErrorSeverity]int{\n\t\tSeverityInfo:     0,\n\t\tSeverityLow:      1,\n\t\tSeverityMedium:   2,\n\t\tSeverityHigh:     3,\n\t\tSeverityCritical: 4,\n\t}\n\tdiff := severityOrder[es] - severityOrder[other]\n\tif diff > 0 {\n\t\treturn 1\n\t} else if diff < 0 {\n\t\treturn -1\n\t}\n\treturn 0\n}\n\n// ErrorSeverityFromString creates an ErrorSeverity from a string.\n// Returns SeverityInfo if the string doesn't match any known severity.\nfunc ErrorSeverityFromString(s string) ErrorSeverity {\n\tswitch ErrorSeverity(strings.ToLower(s)) {\n\tcase SeverityCritical:\n\t\treturn SeverityCritical\n\tcase SeverityHigh:\n\t\treturn SeverityHigh\n\tcase SeverityMedium:\n\t\treturn SeverityMedium\n\tcase SeverityLow:\n\t\treturn SeverityLow\n\tcase SeverityInfo:\n\t\treturn SeverityInfo\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn SeverityInfo\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// DEFAULT SEVERITY MAPPINGS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// defaultSeverityForErrorType defines the default severity for each error type.\nvar defaultSeverityForErrorType = map[string]ErrorSeverity{\n\t// HTTP status codes\n\tErrorTypeStatusCode:          SeverityHigh,\n\tErrorTypeStatusCodeRange:     SeverityHigh,\n\tErrorTypeStatusCodeClass:     SeverityMedium,\n\n\t// Content validation\n\tErrorTypeContentType:         SeverityMedium,\n\tErrorTypeResponseStructure:   SeverityHigh,\n\tErrorTypeResponseBody:        SeverityHigh,\n\tErrorTypeResponseEncoding:    SeverityLow,\n\n\t// Error messages\n\tErrorTypeErrorMessage:        SeverityHigh,\n\tErrorTypeErrorMessagePattern: SeverityMedium,\n\tErrorTypeErrorCode:           SeverityMedium,\n\tErrorTypeErrorDetail:         SeverityLow,\n\n\t// Headers\n\tErrorTypeCORSHeaders:         SeverityMedium,\n\tErrorTypeAuthHeaders:         SeverityCritical,\n\tErrorTypeCustomHeaders:       SeverityLow,\n\n\t// Schema and data validation\n\tErrorTypeJSONSchema:          SeverityHigh,\n\tErrorTypeDataValidation:      SeverityHigh,\n\tErrorTypeFieldValidation:     SeverityMedium,\n\tErrorTypeTypeValidation:      SeverityHigh,\n\n\t// Performance\n\tErrorTypeTimeout:             SeverityHigh,\n\tErrorTypeRateLimit:           SeverityMedium,\n\tErrorTypeRetryExceeded:       SeverityHigh,\n\n\t// Custom\n\tErrorTypeCustom:              SeverityMedium,\n\tErrorTypeUnknown:             SeverityLow,\n}\n\n// GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType returns the default severity for a given error type.\n// Returns SeverityLow if the error type is not recognized.\nfunc GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(errorType string) ErrorSeverity {\n\tif severity, ok := defaultSeverityForErrorType[errorType]; ok {\n\t\treturn severity\n\t}\n\treturn SeverityLow\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERRORTYPE ENUM SEVERITY MAPPINGS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// defaultSeverityForErrorTypeEnum defines the default severity for ErrorType enum values.\n// This mapping is for the ErrorType enum from error_type.go (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat).\nvar defaultSeverityForErrorTypeEnum = map[ErrorType]ErrorSeverity{\n\t// Structural errors (missing fields, wrong types)\n\tErrTypeRequired: SeverityHigh,      // Required field is missing\n\tErrTypeType:     SeverityHigh,      // Wrong type (e.g., string instead of int)\n\tErrTypeLength:   SeverityMedium,    // String/collection length issue\n\n\t// Semantic errors (format, range, value)\n\tErrTypeFormat: SeverityMedium,      // Format doesn't match pattern (e.g., email)\n\tErrTypeRange:  SeverityMedium,      // Value outside numeric range\n\tErrTypeValue:  SeverityLow,         // Value invalid for domain-specific reasons\n\n\t// Constraint errors\n\tErrTypeDuplicate: SeverityHigh,     // Duplicate value (uniqueness constraint)\n\tErrTypeConflict:  SeverityMedium,   // Conflict with other values/constraints\n\n\t// Fallback\n\tErrTypeUnknown: SeverityLow,        // Unknown error type\n}\n\n// GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum returns the default severity for a given ErrorType enum value.\n// Returns SeverityLow if the error type is not recognized.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tseverity := GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(ErrTypeRequired)\n//\t// Returns: SeverityHigh\nfunc GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(errorType ErrorType) ErrorSeverity {\n\tif severity, ok := defaultSeverityForErrorTypeEnum[errorType]; ok {\n\t\treturn severity\n\t}\n\treturn SeverityLow\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERRORTYPE ENUM CATEGORY MAPPINGS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// categoryForErrorTypeEnum defines the category for ErrorType enum values.\n// This mapping is for the ErrorType enum from error_type.go (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat).\nvar categoryForErrorTypeEnum = map[ErrorType]ErrorCategory{\n\t// Structural errors - data structure validation\n\tErrTypeRequired: CategoryValidation, // Required field is missing\n\tErrTypeType:     CategoryValidation, // Value type is incorrect\n\tErrTypeLength:   CategoryValidation, // String is outside acceptable range\n\tErrTypeValue:  CategoryValidation, // Value is invalid\n\n\t// Constraint errors\n\tErrTypeDuplicate: CategoryValidation, // Duplicate value detected\n\tErrTypeConflict:  CategoryValidation, // Conflict with existing values\n\n\t// Fallback\n\tErrTypeUnknown: CategoryCustom, // Unknown error type\n}\n\n// GetCategoryForErrorTypeEnum returns the category for a given CategoryValidation\nfunc GetCategoryForErrorTypeEnum(errorType ErrorType) ErrorCategory {\n\tif category, ok := categoryForErrorTypeEnum[errorType]; ok {\n\t\treturn category\n\t}\n\treturn ErrorFieldGroup represents a collection of validation errors grouped by field name.\n// This type is useful for organizing errors that occur across multiple fields\n// and for presenting field-specific error summaries to users.\ntype ErrorFieldGroup map[string][]ValidationError\n\n// NewErrorFieldGroup creates a new ErrorFieldGroup from a slice of ValidationErrors.\n// Errors are grouped by their FieldName field. Errors without a FieldName are\n// grouped under an empty string key.\nfunc NewErrorFieldGroup(errors []ValidationError) ErrorFieldGroup {\n\tgroup := make(ErrorFieldGroup)\n\tfor _, err := range errors {\n\t\tfieldName := err.FieldName\n\t\tgroup[fieldName] = append(group[fieldName], err)\n\t}\n\treturn group\n}\n\n// Add adds an error to the appropriate field group.\nfunc (efg ErrorFieldGroup) Add(err ValidationError) {\n\tfieldName := err.FieldName\n\tefg[fieldName] = append(efg[fieldName], err)\n}\n\n// GetErrors returns all errors for a specific field.\n// Returns nil if the field has no errors.\nfunc (efg ErrorFieldGroup) GetErrors(fieldName string) []ValidationError {\n\treturn efg[fieldName]\n}\n\n// GetFieldNames returns a list of all field names that have errors.\n// The list is sorted alphabetically for consistent ordering.\nfunc (efg ErrorFieldGroup) GetFieldNames() []string {\n\tnames := make([]string, 0, len(efg))\n\tfor name := range efg {\n\t\tnames = append(names, name)\n\t}\n\treturn names\n}\n\n// HasErrors returns true if the specified field has any errors.\nfunc (efg ErrorFieldGroup) HasErrors(fieldName string) bool {\n\treturn len(efg[fieldName]) > 0\n}\n\n// Count returns the total number of errors across all fields.\nfunc (efg ErrorFieldGroup) Count() int {\n\ttotal := 0\n\tfor _, errors := range efg {\n\t\ttotal += len(errors)\n\t}\n\treturn total\n}\n\n// CountByField returns the number of errors for a specific field.\nfunc (efg ErrorFieldGroup) CountByField(fieldName string) int {\n\treturn len(efg[fieldName])\n}\n\n// FilterBySeverity returns a new ErrorFieldGroup containing only errors\n// of the specified severity or higher.\nfunc (efg ErrorFieldGroup) FilterBySeverity(minSeverity ErrorSeverity) ErrorFieldGroup {\n\tresult := make(ErrorFieldGroup)\n\tfor fieldName, errors := range efg {\n\t\tfor _, err := range errors {\n\t\t\terrSeverity := result\n}\n\n// FilterByCategory returns a new ErrorFieldGroup containing only errors\n// of the specified category.\nfunc (efg ErrorFieldGroup) FilterByCategory(category ErrorCategory) {\n\t\t\t\tresult[fieldName] = append(result[fieldName], err)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn result\n}\n\n// ToMap converts the ErrorFieldGroup to a map[string][]ValidationError.\n// This is useful expected type.\n76\t\t// Example: \"expected number, got string\"\n77\t\tErrTypeType ErrorType = \"type\"\n78\t\n79\t\t// ErrTypeValue indicates a value is invalid for domain-specific validate\n\n/*\nErrorType enum - Common validation error type categories\n\nThis file defines the ErrorType enum, which represents fundamental validation\nerror categories that can be used across different validation contexts. Unlike\nValidationErrorType which is specific to HTTP/API validation, ErrorType provides\ngeneric error type classifications for any validation scenario.\n\nCommon ErrorType values include:\n  - ErrTypeRequired: Required field is missing or empty\n  - ErrTypeFormat: Value format is invalid (e.g., email, UUID pattern)\n  - ErrTypeRange: Value is outside acceptable range (min/max)\n  - ErrTypeLength: String length or collection size is invalid\n  - ErrTypeType: Value type is incorrect (e.g., string when int expected)\n  - ErrTypeValue: Value is invalid for other reasons\n  - ErrTypeDuplicate: Duplicate value detected\n  - ErrTypeConflict: Conflict with existing values or constraints\n  - ErrTypeUnknown: Unknown error type (default/fallback)\n*/\n\nimport (\n\t\"fmt\"\n\t\"strings\"\n)\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR TYPE ENUM\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ErrorType is a strongly-typed enum representing common validation error categories.\n// It provides type safety and prevents typos when specifying error types.\n//\n// ErrorType represents fundamental validation failures that can occur in any\n// validation context, unlike ValidationErrorType which is specific to HTTP/API validation.\n//\n// Use the ErrType* constants (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat) for type-safe\n// error type specification. When you need to use these as strings, use\n// string(ErrTypeRequired) or ErrTypeRequired.String().\n//\n// Usage example:\n//\n//\terr := ValidationError{\n//\t    ErrorType: string(ErrTypeRequired),\n//\t    Message:   \"Field 'email' is required\",\n//\t    FieldName: \"email\",\n//\t}\ntype ErrorType string\n\n// Common validation error type enum values.\nconst (\n\t// ErrTypeRequired indicates a required field is missing or empty.\n\t// Use when a value must be present but is not provided.\n\t// Example: \"email field is required\"\n\tErrTypeRequired ErrorType = \"required\"\n\n\t// ErrTypeFormat indicates a value format is invalid.\n\t// Use when a value doesn't match the expected format pattern.\n\t// Examples: email format, UUID pattern, date format\n\t// Example: \"email format is invalid (must contain @)\"\n\tErrTypeFormat ErrorType = \"format\"\n\n\t// ErrTypeRange indicates a value is outside acceptable numeric range.\n\t// Use when a numeric value invalid.\n\t// Use when a value is too short or too long.\n\t// Example: \"password must be at least 8 characters\"\n\tErrTypeLength ErrorType = \"length\"\n\n\t// ErrTypeType indicates value type is GetCategoryForErrorType(errorType string) ErrorCategory {\n\tif category, ok := errorTypeCategoryMap[errorType]; ok {\n\t\treturn category\n\t}\n\treturn CategoryCustom\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR SEVERITY ENUM\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ErrorSeverity represents the severity level of a validation error.\n// Severity levels help prioritize error handling and user communication.\ntype ErrorSeverity string\n\nconst (\n\t// SeverityCritical indicates a critical error that prevents the system from functioning.\n\t// These errors typically indicate complete failure and require immediate attention.\n\t// Examples: Authentication failures, critical service unavailability.\n\tSeverityCritical ErrorSeverity = \"critical\"\n\n\t// SeverityHigh indicates a high-severity error that significantly impacts functionality.\n\t// These errors prevent core features from working but may have workarounds.\n\t// Examples: Missing required data, invalid authentication tokens.\n\tSeverityHigh ErrorSeverity = \"high\"\n\n\t// SeverityMedium indicates a medium-severity error that partially impacts functionality.\n\t// These errors may affect non-critical features or have mitigations.\n\t// Examples: Optional fields missing, non-critical validation failures.\n\tSeverityMedium ErrorSeverity = \"medium\"\n\n\t// SeverityLow indicates a low-severity error with minimal impact.\n\t// These errors message that doesn't represent a failure.\n\t// These are typically warnings or notices for attention.\n\t// Examples: Deprecation notices, informational messages.\n\tSeverityInfo ErrorSeverity = true\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn false\n\t}\n}\n\n// Description returns a human-readable description of this severity level.\nfunc (es ErrorSeverity) Description() string {\n\tswitch es {\n\tcase SeverityCritical:\n\t\treturn \"Critical error that prevents system functionality\"\n\tcase SeverityHigh:\n\t\treturn \"High-severity error that significantly impacts functionality\"\n\tcase SeverityMedium:\n\t\treturn \"Medium-severity error that partially impacts functionality\"\n\tcase SeverityLow:\n\t\treturn \"Low-severity error with minimal impact\"\n\tcase SeverityInfo:\n\t\treturn \"Informational message that doesn't represent a failure\"\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn \"Unknown severity level\"\n\t}\n}\n\n// IsCritical returns true if this is SeverityCritical.\nfunc (es == SeverityLow || es == SeverityMedium || es == SeverityHigh || es == SeverityCritical\n}\n\n// Compare compares this severity to another severity.\n// Returns:\n//   - positive if this severity is higher\n//   - negative if this severity is lower\n//   - 0 if they are equal\nfunc (es ErrorSeverity) Compare(other ErrorSeverity) int {\n\tseverityOrder := map[ErrorSeverity]int{\n\t\tSeverityInfo:     0,\n\t\tSeverityLow:      1,\n\t\tSeverityMedium:   2,\n\t\tSeverityHigh:     3,\n\t\tSeverityCritical: 4,\n\t}\n\tdiff := severityOrder[es] - severityOrder[other]\n\tif diff > 0 {\n\t\treturn 1\n\t} else if diff < 0 {\n\t\treturn -1\n\t}\n\treturn 0\n}\n\n// ErrorSeverityFromString creates an ErrorSeverity from a string.\n// Returns SeverityInfo if the string doesn't match any known severity.\nfunc ErrorSeverityFromString(s string) ErrorSeverity {\n\tswitch ErrorSeverity(strings.ToLower(s)) {\n\tcase SeverityCritical:\n\t\treturn SeverityCritical\n\tcase SeverityHigh:\n\t\treturn SeverityHigh\n\tcase SeverityMedium:\n\t\treturn SeverityMedium\n\tcase SeverityLow:\n\t\treturn SeverityLow\n\tcase SeverityInfo:\n\t\treturn SeverityInfo\n\tdefault:\n\t\treturn SeverityInfo\n\t}\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// DEFAULT SEVERITY MAPPINGS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// defaultSeverityForErrorType defines the default severity for each error type.\nvar defaultSeverityForErrorType =          SeverityMedium,\n\tErrorTypeRetryExceeded:       SeverityHigh,\n\n\t// Custom\n\tErrorTypeCustom:              SeverityMedium,\n\tErrorTypeUnknown:             SeverityLow,\n}\n\n// GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType returns the default severity for a given error type.\n// Returns SeverityLow if the error type is not recognized.\nfunc GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(errorType string) ErrorSeverity {\n\tif severity, ok := defaultSeverityForErrorType[errorType]; ok {\n\t\treturn severity\n\t}\n\treturn SeverityLow\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERRORTYPE ENUM SEVERITY MAPPINGS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// defaultSeverityForErrorTypeEnum defines the default severity for ErrorType enum values.\n// This mapping is for the ErrorType enum from error_type.go (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat).\nvar defaultSeverityForErrorTypeEnum = map[ErrorType]ErrorSeverity{\n\t// Structural errors (missing fields, wrong types)\n\tErrTypeRequired: SeverityHigh,      // Required field is missing\n\tErrTypeType:     SeverityHigh,      // Wrong type (e.g., string instead of int)\n\tErrTypeLength:   SeverityMedium,    // String/collection length issue\n\n\t// Semantic errors (format, range, value)\n\tErrTypeFormat: SeverityMedium,      // Format doesn't match pattern (e.g., email)\n\tErrTypeRange:  SeverityMedium,      // Value outside numeric range\n\tErrTypeValue:  SeverityLow,         // Value invalid for domain-specific reasons\n\n\t// Constraint errors\n\tErrTypeDuplicate: SeverityHigh,     // Duplicate value (uniqueness constraint)\n\tErrTypeConflict:  SeverityMedium,   // Conflict with other values/constraints\n\n\t// Fallback\n\tErrTypeUnknown: SeverityLow,        // Unknown error type\n}\n\n// GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum returns the default severity for a given ErrorType enum value.\n// Returns SeverityLow if the error type is not recognized.\n//\n// Example usage:\n//\n//\tseverity := GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(ErrTypeRequired)\n//\t// Returns: SeverityHigh\nfunc GetSeverityForErrorTypeEnum(errorType ErrorType) ErrorSeverity {\n\tif severity, ok := defaultSeverityForErrorTypeEnum[errorType]; ok {\n\t\treturn severity\n\t}\n\treturn SeverityLow\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERRORTYPE ENUM CATEGORY MAPPINGS\n// =============================================================================\n\n// categoryForErrorTypeEnum defines the category for ErrorType enum values.\n// This mapping is for the ErrorType enum from error_type.go (e.g., ErrTypeRequired, ErrTypeFormat).\nvar categoryForErrorTypeEnum = map[ErrorType]ErrorCategory{\n\t// Structural errors - data structure validation\n\tErrTypeRequired: CategoryValidation, // Required field is missing\n\tErrTypeType:     CategoryValidation, // Value type is incorrect\n\tErrTypeLength:   CategoryValidation, // String is outside acceptable range\n\tErrTypeValue:  CategoryValidation, // Value is invalid\n\n\t// Constraint errors\n\tErrTypeDuplicate: CategoryValidation, // Duplicate value detected\n\tErrTypeConflict: =============================================================================\n// FIELD-BASED ERROR GROUPING\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ErrorFieldGroup represents a collection of validation errors grouped by field name.\n// This type is useful for organizing errors that occur across multiple fields\n// and for presenting field-specific error summaries to users.\ntype ErrorFieldGroup map[string][]ValidationError\n\n// NewErrorFieldGroup creates a new ErrorFieldGroup from a slice of ValidationErrors.\n// Errors are grouped by their FieldName field. Errors without a FieldName are\n// grouped under an empty string key.\nfunc NewErrorFieldGroup(errors []ValidationError) ErrorFieldGroup {\n\tgroup := make(ErrorFieldGroup)\n\tfor _, err := range errors {\n\t\tfieldName := err.FieldName\n\t\tgroup[fieldName] = append(group[fieldName], err)\n\t}\n\treturn group\n}\n\n// Add adds an error to the appropriate field group.\nfunc (efg ErrorFieldGroup) Add(err ValidationError) {\n\tfieldName := err.FieldName\n\tefg[fieldName] = append(efg[fieldName], err)\n}\n\n// GetErrors returns all errors for a specific field.\n// Returns nil if the field has no errors.\nfunc (efg ErrorFieldGroup) GetErrors(fieldName string) []ValidationError {\n\treturn efg[fieldName]\n}\n\n// GetFieldNames returns a list of all field names that have errors.\n// The list is sorted alphabetically for consistent ordering.\nfunc (efg ErrorFieldGroup) GetFieldNames() []string {\n\tnames := make([]string, 0, len(efg))\n\tfor name := range efg {\n\t\tnames = append(names, name)\n\t}\n\treturn names\n}\n\n// HasErrors returns true if the specified field has any errors.\nfunc (efg ErrorFieldGroup) HasErrors(fieldName string) bool {\n\treturn len(efg[fieldName]) > 0\n}\n\n// Count returns the total number of errors across all fields.\nfunc (efg ErrorFieldGroup) Count() int {\n\ttotal := 0\n\tfor _, errors := range efg {\n\t\ttotal += len(errors)\n\t}\n\treturn total\n}\n\n// CountByField returns the number of errors for a specific field.\nfunc (efg ErrorFieldGroup) CountByField(fieldName string) int {\n\treturn len(efg[fieldName])\n}\n\n// FilterBySeverity returns a new ErrorFieldGroup containing only errors\n// of the specified severity or higher.\nfunc (efg ErrorFieldGroup) FilterBySeverity(minSeverity ErrorSeverity) ErrorFieldGroup {\n\tresult := make(ErrorFieldGroup)\n\tfor fieldName, errors := range efg {\n\t\tfor _, err := range errors {\n\t\t\terrSeverity := result\n}\n\n// FilterByCategory returns a new ErrorFieldGroup containing only errors\n// of the specified category.\nfunc (efg ErrorFieldGroup) FilterByCategory(category ErrorCategory) ErrorFieldGroup {\n\tresult := make(ErrorFieldGroup)\n\tfor fieldName, errors := range efg {\n\t\tfor _, err := range errors {\n\t\t\tif GetCategoryForErrorType(err.ErrorType) == category {\n\t\t\t\tresult[fieldName] = append(result[fieldName], err)\n\t\t\t}\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn result\n}\n\n// ToMap converts the ErrorFieldGroup to a map[string][]ValidationError.\n// This is useful for serialization and external communication.\nfunc (efg ErrorFieldGroup) ToMap() map[string][]ValidationError {\n\treturn map[string][]ValidationError(efg)\n}\n\n// Summary returns a human-readable summary of the field errors.\n// The summary includes field names and error counts.\nfunc (efg ErrorFieldGroup) Summary() string {\n\tvar parts []string\n\tfor fieldName, errors := range efg {\n\t\tif fieldName == \"\" {\n\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"general: %d errors\", len(errors)))\n\t\t} else {\n\t\t\tparts = append(parts, fmt.Sprintf(\"%s: %d errors\", fieldName, len(errors)))\n\t\t}\n\t}\n\treturn strings.Join(parts, \", \")\n}\n\n// =============================================================================\n// ERROR CATEGORY GROUP\n// =============================================================================\n\n// ErrorIndex provides multi-dimensional indexing for validation errors.\n// This type allows efficient filtering and grouping by multiple dimensions:\n// - Field name\n// - Category\n// - Severity\n// - Error type\ntype ErrorIndex struct {\n\tbyField    ErrorFieldGroup\n\tbyCategory ErrorCategoryGroup\n\tbySeverity ErrorSeverityGroup\n\tbyType     map[string][]ValidationError\n\tallErrors  []ValidationError\n}\n\n// NewErrorIndex creates a new ErrorIndex from a slice of ValidationErrors.\nfunc NewErrorIndex(errors []ValidationError) *ErrorIndex {\n\tindex := &ErrorIndex{\n\t\tbyField:    make(ErrorFieldGroup),\n\t\tbyCategory: make(ErrorCategoryGroup),\n\t\tbySeverity: make(ErrorSeverityGroup),\n\t\tbyType:     make(map[string][]ValidationError),\n\t\tallErrors:  errors,\n\t}\n\n\tfor _, err := range errors {\n\t\t// Index by field\n\t\tfieldName := err.FieldName\n\t\tindex.byField[fieldName] = append(index.byField[fieldName], err)\n\n\t\t// Index by category\n\t\tcategory := GetCategoryForErrorType(err.ErrorType)\n\t\tindex.byCategory[category] = append(index.byCategory[category], err)\n\n\t\t// Index by severity\n\t\tseverity := GetDefaultSeverityForErrorType(err.ErrorType)\n\t\tindex.bySeverity[severity] = append(index.bySeverity[severity], err)\n\n\t\t// Index by error type\n\t\tindex.byType[err.ErrorType] = append(index.byType[err.ErrorType], err)\n\t}\n\n\treturn index\n}\n\n// ByField returns the field-based grouping.\nfunc (ei *ErrorIndex) ByField() ErrorFieldGroup {\n\treturn ei.byField\n}\n\n// ByCategory returns the category-based grouping.\nfunc (ei *ErrorIndex) ByCategory() ErrorCategoryGroup {\n\treturn ei.byCategory\n}\n\n// BySeverity returns the severity-based grouping.\nfunc (ei *ErrorIndex) BySeverity() ErrorSeverityGroup {\n\treturn ei.bySeverity\n}\n\n// ByType returns errors grouped by error type.\nfunc (ei *ErrorIndex) ByType() map[string][]ValidationError {\n\treturn ei.byType\n}\n\n// AllErrors returns all errors in the index.\nfunc (ei *ErrorIndex) AllErrors() []ValidationError {\n\treturn ei.allErrors\n}\n\n// FilterByField returns all errors for a specific field.\nfunc (ei *ErrorIndex) FilterByField(fieldName string) []ValidationError {\n\treturn ei.byField.GetErrors(fieldName)\n}\n\n// FilterByCategory returns all errors for a specific category.\nfunc (ei *ErrorIndex) FilterByCategory(category ErrorCategory) []ValidationError {\n\treturn ei.byCategory.GetErrors(category)\n}\n\n// FilterBySeverity returns all errors for a specific severity level.\nfunc (ei *ErrorIndex) FilterBySeverity(severity ErrorSeverity) []ValidationError {\n\treturn ei.bySeverity.GetErrors(severity)\n}\n\n// FilterByTypeError returns all errors for a specific error type.\nfunc (ei *ErrorIndex) FilterByTypeError(errorType string) []ValidationError {\n\treturn ei.byType[errorType]\n}\n\n// HasCriticalErrors returns true if there are any critical errors.\nfunc (ei *ErrorIndex) HasCriticalErrors() bool {\n\treturn ei.bySeverity.HasCriticalErrors()\n}\n\n// HasHighOrCriticalErrors returns true if there are any high or critical errors.\nfunc (ei *ErrorIndex) HasHighOrCriticalErrors() bool {\n\treturn ei.bySeverity.HasHighOrCriticalErrors()\n}\n\n// Count returns the total number of errors in the index.\nfunc (ei *ErrorIndex) Count() int {\n\treturn len(ei.allErrors)\n}\n\n// Summary returns a comprehensive summary of all errors.\nfunc (ei *ErrorIndex) Summary() string {\n\treturn fmt.Sprintf(\"Total: %d errors | By field: %s | By category: %s | By severity: %s\",\n\t\tei.Count(),\n\t\tei.byField.Summary(),\n\t\tei.byCategory.Summary(),\n\t\tei.bySeverity.Summary(),\n\t)\n}\n","structuredPatch":[{"oldStart":180,"oldLines":10,"newStart":180,"newLines":20,"lines":["
